{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_036a63918fdb46a992cae574","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\t/*\n4\tPackage validate provides a standardized, extensible validation error format for HTTP API testing.\n5\t\n6\tThis package defines the ValidationError data structure, which offers a comprehensive, machine-readable\n7\tformat for representing validation failures across different types of API validations (HTTP status codes,\n8\terror message patterns, content types, response structures, CORS headers, and more).\n9\t\n10\t# Validation Error Format\n11\t\n12\tThe ValidationError struct is the core of this package's error reporting system. It follows these design\n13\tprinciples:\n14\t\n15\t  - Serializable: Full JSON marshaling/unmarshaling support via struct tags\n16\t  - Machine-readable: Clear field names suitable for automated processing and log aggregation\n17\t  - Human-readable: Descriptive messages with helpful suggestions for debugging\n18\t  - Extensible: Optional fields provide additional context without breaking existing code\n19\t\n20\t# Required vs Optional Fields\n21\t\n22\tRequired fields that must be set for every ValidationError:\n23\t  - ErrorType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n24\t  - Message: A concise, human-readable description of the validation failure\n25\t\n26\tOptional fields that provide additional context:\n27\t  - Expected/Actual: The values that were expected and actually received\n28\t  - Context: Where/when the validation occurred (e.g., endpoint, operation type)\n29\t  - FieldName: Specific field where the error was found (e.g., in response body JSON)\n30\t  - Location: Position information (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\")\n31\t  - RelatedFields: Other fields related to this error for additional context\n32\t  - PatternDetails: Information about regex pattern matching failures\n33\t  - RangeInfo: Range boundaries for range-based validations (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n34\t  - ValidationDetails: Additional validation-specific information as a list\n35\t  - ResponseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging\n36\t  - Suggestions: Actionable recommendations for resolving the error\n37\t\n38\t# Creating Validation Errors\n39\t\n40\tThis package provides several ways to create ValidationError instances:\n41\t\n42\t  1. Direct struct initialization: Create ValidationError directly for full control\n43\t  2. ValidationFormatter builder: Use the builder pattern for ergonomic, fluent construction\n44\t  3. Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n45\t  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n46\t\n47\tExample usage:\n48\t\n49\t\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n50\t\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n51\t\t\tWithExpected(200).\n52\t\t\tWithActual(404).\n53\t\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n54\t\t\tFormat()\n55\t\t// Output:\n56\t\t// status_code validation failed\n57\t\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n58\t\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n59\t\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n60\t\t//   Suggestions:\n61\t\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n62\t\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n63\t\n64\t\t// Using a convenience function\n65\t\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n66\t\n67\t# Error Interface\n68\t\n69\tValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\n70\tthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\n71\twith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n72\t\n73\t# Serialization\n74\t\n75\tValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n76\t\n77\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n78\t\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n79\t\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n80\t\n81\tThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\n82\tmanipulation before serialization.\n83\t\n84\t# Validation\n85\t\n86\tThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\n87\trequired fields populated. Use this to catch programming errors early:\n88\t\n89\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n90\t\tif err := err.Validate(); err != nil {\n91\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"Invalid error structure: %v\", err)\n92\t\t}\n93\t*/\n94\t\n95\timport (\n96\t\t\"fmt\"\n97\t\t\"strings\"\n98\t)\n99\t\n100\t// =============================================================================\n101\t// HELPER TYPES\n102\t// =============================================================================\n103\t\n104\t// ValidationErrorData is a simplified, serialization-friendly version of ValidationError.\n105\t// It provides a consistent format for external communication and API responses.\n106\t// This type is designed to be easily marshaled to JSON and consumed by external systems.\n107\t//\n108\t// Relationship to ValidationError:\n109\t// - ValidationError is the internal, comprehensive error structure used by the validate package\n110\t// - ValidationErrorData is the external, simplified format returned to API clients\n111\t// - Use ToValidationErrorData() to convert ValidationError to ValidationErrorData\n112\t//\n113\t// # Required Fields\n114\t//\n115\t// The MessageType, ErrorType, and Message fields are required for all ValidationErrorData.\n116\t//\n117\t// # Optional Fields\n118\t//\n119\t// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the specific validation scenario.\n120\t//\n121\t// Example usage:\n122\t//\n123\t//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n124\t//\t    MessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n125\t//\t    ErrorType:    \"status_code\",\n126\t//\t    Message:      \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n127\t//\t    Context:      \"GET /api/users/123\",\n128\t//\t    Expected:     200,\n129\t//\t    Actual:       404,\n130\t//\t    Suggestions:  []string{\"Check the endpoint URL\", \"Verify resource exists\"},\n131\t//\t}\n132\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\n133\ttype ValidationErrorData struct {\n134\t\t// MessageType is a fixed identifier for this type of error message.\n135\t\t// Always \"validation_failed\" for ValidationErrorData instances.\n136\t\t// This field allows clients to quickly identify the error category.\n137\t\tMessageType string `json:\"message_type\"`\n138\t\n139\t\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n140\t\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n141\t\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n142\t\t// Use the ErrorType* constants for type-safe error type specification.\n143\t\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n144\t\n145\t\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n146\t\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n147\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n148\t\n149\t\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n150\t\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n151\t\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n152\t\n153\t\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n154\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n155\t\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n156\t\n157\t\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n158\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n159\t\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n160\t\n161\t\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n162\t\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n163\t\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n164\t\n165\t\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n166\t\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n167\t\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n168\t\n169\t\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n170\t\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n171\t\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n172\t\n173\t\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n174\t\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n175\t\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n176\t\n177\t\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n178\t\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n179\t\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n180\t\n181\t\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n182\t\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n183\t\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n184\t\n185\t\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n186\t\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n187\t\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n188\t\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n189\t\n190\t\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n191\t\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n192\t\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n193\t\n194\t\t// Category is the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n195\t\t// Categories group related error types together for easier handling and filtering.\n196\t\tCategory ErrorCategory `json:\"category,omitempty\"`\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t// Error implements the error interface for ValidationErrorData.\n200\t// It returns the Message field for compatibility with error handling patterns.\n201\t//\n202\t// Example usage:\n203\t//\n204\t//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{Message: \"Validation failed\"}\n205\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Error: %v\", data)\n206\tfunc (ve ValidationErrorData) Error() string {\n207\t\treturn ve.Message\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\t// ToValidationErrorData converts a ValidationError to ValidationErrorData format.\n211\t// This function creates a simplified, serialization-friendly version of the validation error\n212\t// suitable for external communication and API responses.\n213\t//\n214\t// The conversion maps ValidationError fields to ValidationErrorData fields,\n215\t// with the following special handling:\n216\t// - ErrorType is directly copied\n217\t// - Message is generated if empty\n218\t// - MessageType is always set to \"validation_failed\"\n219\t// - All other fields are preserved if present\n220\t//\n221\t// Parameters:\n222\t//   - ve: The ValidationError to convert\n223\t//\n224\t// Returns a ValidationErrorData instance with populated fields.\n225\t//\n226\t// Example usage:\n227\t//\n228\t//\tve := ValidationError{\n229\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n230\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n231\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n232\t//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n233\t//\t}\n234\t//\tdata := ToValidationErrorData(ve)\n235\t//\t// data.MessageType = \"validation_failed\"\n236\t//\t// data.ErrorType = \"status_code\"\n237\t//\t// data.Context = \"GET /api/users\"\n238\tfunc ToValidationErrorData(ve ValidationError) ValidationErrorData {\n239\t\t// Generate message if not provided\n240\t\tmessage := ve.Message\n241\t\tif message == \"\" {\n242\t\t\t// Generate detailed message with expected/actual values\n243\t\t\tmessage = generateValidationMessage(ve.ErrorType, ve.Expected, ve.Actual)\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n247\t\t\tMessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n248\t\t\tErrorType:    ve.ErrorType,\n249\t\t\tMessage:      message,\n250\t\t\tContext:      ve.Context,\n251\t\t\tExpected:     ve.Expected,\n252\t\t\tActual:       ve.Actual,\n253\t\t\tFieldName:    ve.FieldName,\n254\t\t\tLocation:     ve.Location,\n255\t\t\tRelatedFields:     ve.RelatedFields,\n256\t\t\tPatternDetails:    ve.PatternDetails,\n257\t\t\tRangeInfo:         ve.RangeInfo,\n258\t\t\tValidationDetails: ve.ValidationDetails,\n259\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   ve.ResponseSnippet,\n260\t\t\tSuggestions:       ve.Suggestions,\n261\t\t\tCategory:          ve.Category,\n262\t\t}\n263\t\n264\t\treturn data\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t// generateValidationMessage creates a detailed validation message with expected/actual values.\n268\tfunc generateValidationMessage(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}) string {\n269\t\tvar parts []string\n270\t\n271\t\t// Add expected value details\n272\t\tif expected != nil {\n273\t\t\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\n274\t\t\tcase int:\n275\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n276\t\t\tcase []int:\n277\t\t\t\texpectedStr := \"one of [\"\n278\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n279\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n280\t\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \", \"\n281\t\t\t\t\t}\n282\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n283\t\t\t\t}\n284\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \"]\"\n285\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, expectedStr)\n286\t\t\tcase string:\n287\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected '%s'\", exp))\n288\t\t\tdefault:\n289\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %v\", exp))\n290\t\t\t}\n291\t\t}\n292\t\n293\t\t// Add actual value details\n294\t\tif actual != nil {\n295\t\t\tswitch act := actual.(type) {\n296\t\t\tcase int:\n297\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n298\t\t\tcase string:\n299\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n300\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n301\t\t\t\t}\n302\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got '%s'\", act))\n303\t\t\tdefault:\n304\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %v\", act))\n305\t\t\t}\n306\t\t}\n307\t\n308\t\t// Combine parts\n309\t\tif len(parts) > 0 {\n310\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed: %s\", errorType, strings.Join(parts, \" \"))\n311\t\t}\n312\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", errorType)\n313\t}\n314\t\n315\t// ValidationError is the core data structure for validation error representation.\n316\t// It provides a standardized format for validation errors across different\n317\t// validation types (status codes, error messages, content types, etc.).\n318\t//\n319\t// This structure is designed to be:\n320\t// - Serializable: Includes JSON tags for marshaling/unmarshaling\n321\t// - Machine-readable: Clear field names for automated processing\n322\t// - Human-readable: Descriptive messages for debugging\n323\t// - Extensible: Optional fields for additional context\n324\t//\n325\t// # Required Fields\n326\t//\n327\t// The Message and ErrorType fields are required for all validation errors.\n328\t//\n329\t// # Optional Fields\n330\t//\n331\t// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the\n332\t// specific validation scenario.\n333\t//\n334\t// Example usage:\n335\t//\n336\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n337\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code_mismatch\",\n338\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n339\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n340\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n341\t//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n342\t//\t}\n343\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n344\ttype ValidationError struct {\n345\t\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n346\t\t// This field identifies what kind of validation was performed and failed.\n347\t\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n348\t\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n349\t\t//\n350\t\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n351\t\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n352\t\n353\t\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n354\t\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n355\t\t// The message should be concise but informative enough for debugging.\n356\t\t//\n357\t\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n358\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n359\t\n360\t\t// Category is the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n361\t\t// Categories group related error types together for easier handling and filtering.\n362\t\t// Common values include: \"http\", \"content\", \"validation\", \"performance\", \"security\", \"success\", \"custom\".\n363\t\t//\n364\t\t// This field is optional and will be auto-derived from ErrorType if not set.\n365\t\tCategory ErrorCategory `json:\"category,omitempty\"`\n366\t\n367\t\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n368\t\t// This can include endpoint names, operation types, or other contextual details.\n369\t\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n370\t\t// This field is optional and can be empty if no context is available.\n371\t\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n372\t\n373\t\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n374\t\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n375\t\t// When present, it helps identify the discrepancy between expected and actual values.\n376\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n377\t\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n378\t\n379\t\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n380\t\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n381\t\t// When present, it shows the specific value that caused the validation failure.\n382\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n383\t\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n384\t\n385\t\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n386\t\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n387\t\t// This field is optional.\n388\t\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n389\t\n390\t\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n391\t\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n392\t\t// This field is optional.\n393\t\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n394\t\n395\t\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n396\t\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n397\t\t// This field is optional.\n398\t\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n399\t\n400\t\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n401\t\t// This is useful when validating error messages against regex patterns.\n402\t\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n403\t\t// This field is optional.\n404\t\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n405\t\n406\t\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n407\t\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n408\t\t// This field is optional.\n409\t\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n410\t\n411\t\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n412\t\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n413\t\t// This field is optional.\n414\t\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n415\t\n416\t\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n417\t\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n418\t\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n419\t\t// This field is optional.\n420\t\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n421\t\n422\t\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n423\t\t// This field is optional and contains hints or steps to fix the issue.\n424\t\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n425\t\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n426\t}\n427\t\n428\t// Error implements the error interface for ValidationError.\n429\t// It returns a detailed, multi-line error message that includes all relevant\n430\t// validation information for debugging and resolution.\n431\t//\n432\t// The output includes:\n433\t// - The validation type\n434\t// - Expected vs actual values\n435\t// - Context (if provided)\n436\t// - Field name (if provided)\n437\t// - Location (if provided)\n438\t// - Related fields (if provided)\n439\t// - Pattern details (if provided)\n440\t// - Range info (if provided)\n441\t// - Validation details (if provided)\n442\t// - Response snippet (if provided)\n443\t// - Suggestions for fixing the issue\n444\t//\n445\t// Example usage:\n446\t//\n447\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n448\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n449\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n450\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n451\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n452\t//\t}\n453\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n454\tfunc (ve ValidationError) Error() string {\n455\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n456\t\n457\t\t// Use the Message field as the first line if it's different from the default\n458\t\tif ve.Message != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(ve.Message, ve.ErrorType+\" validation failed\") {\n459\t\t\tb.WriteString(ve.Message)\n460\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n461\t\t} else {\n462\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\\n\", ve.ErrorType))\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\t// Expected vs Actual\n466\t\tif ve.Expected != nil || ve.Actual != nil {\n467\t\t\t// For status code validation, use \"Expected:\" and \"Received:\" labels\n468\t\t\tisStatusCode := ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\"\n469\t\n470\t\t\tswitch exp := ve.Expected.(type) {\n471\t\t\tcase int:\n472\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n473\t\t\tcase []int:\n474\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Expected: one of [\")\n475\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n476\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n477\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n478\t\t\t\t\t}\n479\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n480\t\t\t\t}\n481\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n482\t\t\tcase string:\n483\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %s\\n\", exp))\n484\t\t\tdefault:\n485\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %v\\n\", exp))\n486\t\t\t}\n487\t\n488\t\t\t// Actual value - use \"Received:\" for status code validation\n489\t\t\tswitch act := ve.Actual.(type) {\n490\t\t\tcase int:\n491\t\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n492\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n493\t\t\t\t} else {\n494\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n495\t\t\t\t}\n496\t\t\tcase string:\n497\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n498\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n499\t\t\t\t}\n500\t\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n501\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %s\\n\", act))\n502\t\t\t\t} else {\n503\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %s\\n\", act))\n504\t\t\t\t}\n505\t\t\tdefault:\n506\t\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n507\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %v\\n\", act))\n508\t\t\t\t} else {\n509\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %v\\n\", act))\n510\t\t\t\t}\n511\t\t\t}\n512\t\t}\n513\t\n514\t\t// Context - use \"Request:\" for status code validation\n515\t\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n516\t\t\tif ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\" {\n517\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Request:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n518\t\t\t} else {\n519\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n520\t\t\t}\n521\t\t}\n522\t\n523\t\t// Field name\n524\t\tif ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n525\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field:    %s\\n\", ve.FieldName))\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t// Location\n529\t\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n530\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: %s\\n\", ve.Location))\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\t// Related fields\n534\t\tif len(ve.RelatedFields) > 0 {\n535\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Related fields: [\")\n536\t\t\tfor i, field := range ve.RelatedFields {\n537\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n538\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n539\t\t\t\t}\n540\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(field)\n541\t\t\t}\n542\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n543\t\t}\n544\t\n545\t\t// Pattern details\n546\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(ve.PatternDetails) != \"\" {\n547\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Pattern:  %s\\n\", ve.PatternDetails))\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t// Range info\n551\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(ve.RangeInfo) != \"\" {\n552\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Range:    %s\\n\", ve.RangeInfo))\n553\t\t}\n554\t\n555\t\t// Validation details\n556\t\tif len(ve.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n557\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Details:\\n\")\n558\t\t\tfor _, detail := range ve.ValidationDetails {\n559\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", detail))\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\t// Response snippet\n564\t\tif ve.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n565\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Response: %s\\n\", ve.ResponseSnippet))\n566\t\t}\n567\t\n568\t\t// Common causes section - provide context for common failure scenarios\n569\t\tif len(ve.Suggestions) > 0 {\n570\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Common causes:\\n\")\n571\t\t\tswitch ve.ErrorType {\n572\t\t\tcase \"status_code\":\n573\t\t\t\tif statusCode, ok := ve.Actual.(int); ok {\n574\t\t\t\t\tswitch statusCode {\n575\t\t\t\t\tcase 404:\n576\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Resource does not exist or has been moved\\n\")\n577\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Incorrect endpoint URL or resource identifier\\n\")\n578\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Insufficient permissions to access the resource\\n\")\n579\t\t\t\t\tcase 401:\n580\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Missing or invalid authentication credentials\\n\")\n581\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Expired or revoked API token/session\\n\")\n582\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Incorrect authentication method for this endpoint\\n\")\n583\t\t\t\t\tcase 403:\n584\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Insufficient permissions or scopes for this resource\\n\")\n585\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Account restrictions or quota limits\\n\")\n586\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - IP whitelist or geographic restrictions\\n\")\n587\t\t\t\t\tcase 500:\n588\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Internal server error or service malfunction\\n\")\n589\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Temporary service unavailability or maintenance\\n\")\n590\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Upstream service or database connectivity issues\\n\")\n591\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n592\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Request or response format mismatch\\n\")\n593\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Server-side validation or processing error\\n\")\n594\t\t\t\t\t}\n595\t\t\t\t}\n596\t\t\tcase \"error_message\":\n597\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Pattern mismatch between expected and actual error messages\\n\")\n598\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - API behavior change or version incompatibility\\n\")\n599\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Conditional error paths not triggered as expected\\n\")\n600\t\t\tcase \"content_type\":\n601\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - API returns different content type than expected\\n\")\n602\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Missing or incorrect Content-Type header\\n\")\n603\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Error response returned in unexpected format\\n\")\n604\t\t\tcase \"timeout\":\n605\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Request processing time exceeded configured timeout\\n\")\n606\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Network latency or connectivity issues\\n\")\n607\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Server-side performance degradation\\n\")\n608\t\t\tcase \"json_schema\":\n609\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Response structure doesn't match expected schema\\n\")\n610\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Missing or incorrectly typed fields in response\\n\")\n611\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - API contract change or version mismatch\\n\")\n612\t\t\tcase \"status_code_range\":\n613\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Response status code falls outside expected range\\n\")\n614\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Unexpected success or error condition\\n\")\n615\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Test expectations may need updating\\n\")\n616\t\t\tcase \"cors_headers\":\n617\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - CORS headers not properly configured on server\\n\")\n618\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Origin not allowed in CORS policy\\n\")\n619\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Pre-flight request validation failed\\n\")\n620\t\t\tdefault:\n621\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Validation condition not met for this check\\n\")\n622\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Expected and actual values don't match\\n\")\n623\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"    - Configuration or environment issue\\n\")\n624\t\t\t}\n625\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n626\t\t}\n627\t\n628\t\t// Suggestions - format with numbered list for better readability\n629\t\tif len(ve.Suggestions) > 0 {\n630\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Suggestions:\\n\")\n631\t\t\tfor i, suggestion := range ve.Suggestions {\n632\t\t\t\t// Don't add trailing newline after last suggestion\n633\t\t\t\tif i == len(ve.Suggestions)-1 {\n634\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %d. %s\", i+1, suggestion))\n635\t\t\t\t} else {\n636\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %d. %s\\n\", i+1, suggestion))\n637\t\t\t\t}\n638\t\t\t}\n639\t\t}\n640\t\n641\t\treturn b.String()\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t// getStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code.\n645\t// This is a lightweight version for error formatting.\n646\tfunc getStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n647\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n648\t\t\t100: \"Continue\", 101: \"Switching Protocols\", 102: \"Processing\",\n649\t\t\t200: \"OK\", 201: \"Created\", 202: \"Accepted\", 203: \"Non-Authoritative json.Marshal(data)\n696\tfunc (ve ValidationError) ToMap() map[string]interface{} {\n697\t\tresult := map[string]interface{}{\n698\t\t\t\"error_type\": ve.ErrorType,\n699\t\t\t\"message\": ve.Expected\n707\t\t}\n708\t\tif ve.Actual != nil {\n709\t\t\tresult[\"actual\"] = ve.Actual\n710\t\t}\n711\t\tif ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n712\t\t\tresult[\"field_name\"] = ve.FieldName\n713\t\t}\n714\t\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n715\t\t\tresult[\"location\"] = ve.Location\n716\t\t}\n717\t\tif len(ve.RelatedFields) > 0 {\n718\t\t\tresult[\"related_fields\"] = validate\n\n/*\nPackage validate provides a standardized, extensible validation error format for HTTP API testing.\n\nThis package defines the ValidationError data structure, which offers a comprehensive, machine-readable\nformat for representing validation failures across different types of API validations (HTTP status codes,\nerror message patterns, content types, response structures, CORS headers, and more).\n\n# Validation Error Format\n\nThe ValidationError struct is the core of this package's error reporting system. It follows these design\nprinciples:\n\n  - Serializable: Full JSON marshaling/unmarshaling support via struct tags\n  - Machine-readable: Clear field names suitable for automated processing and log aggregation\n  - Human-readable: Descriptive messages with helpful suggestions for debugging\n  - Extensible: Optional fields provide additional context without breaking existing code\n\n# Required vs Optional Fields\n\nRequired fields that must be set for every ValidationError:\n  - ErrorType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n  - Message: A concise, human-readable description of the validation failure\n\nOptional fields that provide additional context:\n  - Expected/Actual: The values that were expected and actually received\n  - Context: Where/when the validation occurred (e.g., endpoint, operation type)\n  - FieldName: Specific field where the error was found (e.g., in response body JSON)\n  - Location: Position information (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\")\n  - RelatedFields: Other fields related to this error for additional context\n  - PatternDetails: Information about regex pattern matching failures\n  - RangeInfo: Range boundaries for range-based validations (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n  - ValidationDetails: Additional validation-specific information as a list\n  - ResponseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging\n  - Suggestions: Actionable recommendations for resolving the error\n\n# Creating Validation Errors\n\nThis package provides several ways to create ValidationError instances:\n\n  1. Direct struct initialization: Create ValidationError directly for full control\n  2. ValidationFormatter builder: Use the builder pattern for ergonomic, fluent construction\n  3. Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n\nExample usage:\n\n\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(200).\n\t\tWithActual(404).\n\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n\t\tFormat()\n\t// Output:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n\t//   Suggestions:\n\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n\n\t// Using a convenience function\n\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n\n# Error Interface\n\nValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\nthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\nwith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n\n# Serialization\n\nValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n\n\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n\nThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\nmanipulation before serialization.\n\n# Validation\n\nThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\nrequired fields populated. Use this to catch programming errors early:\n\n\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n\tif err := err.Validate(); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"Invalid error structure: %v\", err)\n\t}\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidationErrorData is a simplified, serialization-friendly version of ValidationError.\n// It provides a consistent format for external communication and API responses.\n// This type is designed to be easily marshaled to JSON and consumed by external systems.\n//\n// Relationship to ValidationError:\n// - ValidationError is the internal, comprehensive error structure used by the validate package\n// - ValidationErrorData is the external, simplified format returned to API clients\n// - Use ToValidationErrorData() to convert ValidationError to ValidationErrorData\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// The MessageType, ErrorType, and Message fields are required for all ValidationErrorData.\n//\n// # Optional Fields\n//\n// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the specific validation scenario.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n//\t    MessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n//\t    ErrorType:    \"status_code\",\n//\t    Message:      \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Context:      \"GET /api/users/123\",\n//\t    Expected:     200,\n//\t    Actual:       404,\n//\t    Suggestions:  []string{\"Check the endpoint URL\", \"Verify resource exists\"},\n//\t}\n//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\ntype ValidationErrorData struct {\n\t// MessageType is a fixed identifier for this type of error message.\n\t// Always \"validation_failed\" for ValidationErrorData instances.\n\t// This field allows clients to quickly identify the error category.\n\tMessageType string `json:\"message_type\"`\n\n\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n\t// Use the ErrorType* constants for type-safe error type specification.\n\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\n\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Category is the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n\t// Categories group related error types together for easier handling and filtering.\n\tCategory ErrorCategory `json:\"category,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface for ValidationErrorData.\n// It returns the Message field for compatibility with error handling patterns.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{Message: \"Validation failed\"}\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Error: %v\", data)\nfunc (ve ValidationErrorData) Error() string {\n\treturn ve.Message\n}\n\n// ToValidationErrorData converts a ValidationError to ValidationErrorData format.\n// This function creates a simplified, serialization-friendly version of the validation error\n// suitable for external communication and API responses.\n//\n// The conversion maps ValidationError fields to ValidationErrorData fields,\n// with the following special handling:\n// - ErrorType is directly copied\n// - Message is generated if empty\n// - MessageType is always set to \"validation_failed\"\n// - All other fields are preserved if present\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ve: The ValidationError to convert\n//\n// Returns a ValidationErrorData instance with populated fields.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tve := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n//\t}\n//\tdata := ToValidationErrorData(ve)\n//\t// data.MessageType = \"validation_failed\"\n//\t// data.ErrorType = \"status_code\"\n//\t// data.Context = \"GET /api/users\"\nfunc ToValidationErrorData(ve ValidationError) ValidationErrorData {\n\t// Generate message if not provided\n\tmessage := ve.Message\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Generate detailed message with expected/actual values\n\t\tmessage = generateValidationMessage(ve.ErrorType, ve.Expected, ve.Actual)\n\t}\n\n\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n\t\tMessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n\t\tErrorType:    ve.ErrorType,\n\t\tMessage:      message,\n\t\tContext:      ve.Context,\n\t\tExpected:     ve.Expected,\n\t\tActual:       ve.Actual,\n\t\tFieldName:    ve.FieldName,\n\t\tLocation:     ve.Location,\n\t\tRelatedFields:     ve.RelatedFields,\n\t\tPatternDetails:    ve.PatternDetails,\n\t\tRangeInfo:         ve.RangeInfo,\n\t\tValidationDetails: ve.ValidationDetails,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   ve.ResponseSnippet,\n\t\tSuggestions:       ve.Suggestions,\n\t\tCategory:          ve.Category,\n\t}\n\n\treturn data\n}\n\n// generateValidationMessage creates a detailed validation message with expected/actual values.\nfunc generateValidationMessage(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\n\t// Add expected value details\n\tif expected != nil {\n\t\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\texpectedStr := \"one of [\"\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \", \"\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\texpectedStr += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr += \"]\"\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, expectedStr)\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected '%s'\", exp))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %v\", exp))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add actual value details\n\tif actual != nil {\n\t\tswitch act := actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got '%s'\", act))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %v\", act))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine parts\n\tif len(parts) > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed: %s\", errorType, strings.Join(parts, \" \"))\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", errorType)\n}\n\n// ValidationError is the core data structure for validation error representation.\n// It provides a standardized format for validation errors across different\n// validation types (status codes, error messages, content types, etc.).\n//\n// This structure is designed to be:\n// - Serializable: Includes JSON tags for marshaling/unmarshaling\n// - Machine-readable: Clear field names for automated processing\n// - Human-readable: Descriptive messages for debugging\n// - Extensible: Optional fields for additional context\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// The Message and ErrorType fields are required for all validation errors.\n//\n// # Optional Fields\n//\n// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the\n// specific validation scenario.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code_mismatch\",\n//\t    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n//\t}\n//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n\t// This field identifies what kind of validation was performed and failed.\n\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n\t//\n\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n\t// The message should be concise but informative enough for debugging.\n\t//\n\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\n\t// Category is the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n\t// Categories group related error types together for easier handling and filtering.\n\t// Common values include: \"http\", \"content\", \"validation\", \"performance\", \"security\", \"success\", \"custom\".\n\t//\n\t// This field is optional and will be auto-derived from ErrorType if not set.\n\tCategory ErrorCategory `json:\"category,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n\t// This can include endpoint names, operation types, or other contextual details.\n\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n\t// This field is optional and can be empty if no context is available.\n\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n\t// When present, it helps identify the discrepancy between expected and actual values.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n\t// When present, it shows the specific value that caused the validation failure.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n\t// This is useful when validating error messages against regex patterns.\n\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n\t// This field is optional and contains hints or steps to fix the issue.\n\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface for ValidationError.\n// It returns a detailed, multi-line error message that includes all relevant\n// validation information for debugging and resolution.\n//\n// The output includes:\n// - The validation type\n// - Expected vs actual values\n// - Context (if provided)\n// - Field name (if provided)\n// - Location (if provided)\n// - Related fields (if provided)\n// - Pattern details (if provided)\n// - Range info (if provided)\n// - Validation details (if provided)\n// - Response snippet (if provided)\n// - Suggestions for fixing the issue\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t}\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\nfunc (ve ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\t// Use the Message field as the first line if it's different from the default\n\tif ve.Message != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(ve.Message, ve.ErrorType+\" validation failed\") {\n\t\tb.WriteString(ve.Message)\n\t\tb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\\n\", ve.ErrorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Expected vs Actual\n\tif ve.Expected != nil || ve.Actual != nil {\n\t\t// For status code validation, use \"Expected:\" and \"Received:\" labels\n\t\tisStatusCode := ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\"\n\n\t\tswitch exp := ve.Expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Expected: one of [\")\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %s\\n\", exp))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %v\\n\", exp))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Actual value - use \"Received:\" for status code validation\n\t\tswitch act := ve.Actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %s\\n\", act))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %s\\n\", act))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tif isStatusCode
