{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a17980cc4033471883135bf5","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"strings\"\n6\t\t\"sync\"\n7\t)\n8\t\n9\t// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n10\t//\n11\t// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n12\t// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n13\t//\n14\t// # Design Pattern\n15\t//\n16\t// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n17\t//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n18\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n19\t//   - Type-safe error construction\n20\t//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n21\t//\n22\t// # Required Fields\n23\t//\n24\t// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n25\t// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n26\t// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n27\t//\n28\t// # Common Usage Pattern\n29\t//\n30\t// The typical workflow is:\n31\t//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n32\t//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n33\t//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n34\t//\n35\t// # Example\n36\t//\n37\t//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n38\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n39\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n40\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n41\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n42\t//\t    Format()\n43\t//\n44\t//\t// With custom suggestions\n45\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n46\t//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n47\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n48\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n49\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n50\t//\t    Format()\n51\t//\n52\t// # Extending for New Validation Types\n53\t//\n54\t// To add support for a new validation type:\n55\t//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n56\t//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n57\t//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n58\t//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n59\t//\n60\t// # Fields\n61\t//\n62\t// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n63\t// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\n64\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n65\t\tvalidationType string\n66\t\texpected       interface{}\n67\t\tactual         interface{}\n68\t\tcontext        string\n69\t\tresponseSnippet string\n70\t\tfieldName      string\n71\t\tpatternDetails string\n72\t\trangeInfo      string\n73\t\tvalidationDetails []string\n74\t\tcustomSuggestions []string\n75\t\tcategory       ErrorCategory\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n79\t//\n80\t// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n81\t// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n82\t//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n83\t//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n84\t//   - Provide context for error handling\n85\t//\n86\t// # Parameters\n87\t//\n88\t//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n89\t//\n90\t//     Common validation types include:\n91\t//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n92\t//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n93\t//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n94\t//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n95\t//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n96\t//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n97\t//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n98\t//\n99\t// # Returns\n100\t//\n101\t// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n102\t//\n103\t// # Example Usage\n104\t//\n105\t//\t// Basic status code validation\n106\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n107\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n108\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n109\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n110\t//\t    Format()\n111\t//\n112\t//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n113\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n114\t//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n115\t//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n116\t//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n117\t//\t    Format()\n118\t//\n119\t//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n120\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n121\t//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n122\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n123\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n124\t//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n125\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n126\t//\t    Format()\n127\tfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n128\t\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n129\t\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n130\t\t}\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n134\t//\n135\t// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n136\t// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n137\t// error messages showing what went wrong.\n138\t//\n139\t// # Parameters\n140\t//\n141\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n142\t//\n143\t//     Common types:\n144\t//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n145\t//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n146\t//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n147\t//\n148\t// # Returns\n149\t//\n150\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n151\t//\n152\t// # Example\n153\t//\n154\t//\t// Single expected value\n155\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n156\t//\n157\t//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n158\t//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n159\t//\n160\t//\t// String pattern\n161\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\n162\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n163\t\tvf.expected = expected\n164\t\treturn vf\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n168\t//\n169\t// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n170\t// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n171\t// that caused the validation failure.\n172\t//\n173\t// # Parameters\n174\t//\n175\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n176\t//\n177\t//     Common types:\n178\t//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n179\t//     - string: Actual message or content received\n180\t//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n181\t//\n182\t// # Returns\n183\t//\n184\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n185\t//\n186\t// # Example\n187\t//\n188\t//\t// Status code that was received\n189\t//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n190\t//\n191\t//\t// Error message that was received\n192\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n193\t//\n194\t//\t// Content-Type that was received\n195\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\n196\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n197\t\tvf.actual = actual\n198\t\treturn vf\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n202\t//\n203\t// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n204\t// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n205\t// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n206\t//\n207\t// # Parameters\n208\t//\n209\t//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n210\t//\n211\t//     Common context examples:\n212\t//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n213\t//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n214\t//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n215\t//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n216\t//\n217\t// # Returns\n218\t//\n219\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n220\t//\n221\t// # Example\n222\t//\n223\t//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n224\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n225\t//\n226\t//\t// Operation context\n227\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n228\t//\n229\t//\t// Test scenario context\n230\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\n231\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n232\t\tvf.context = context\n233\t\treturn vf\n234\t}\n235\t\n236\t// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n237\t//\n238\t// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n239\t// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n240\t// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n241\t//\n242\t// # Parameters\n243\t//\n244\t//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n245\t//\n246\t//     Best practices:\n247\t//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n248\t//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n249\t//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n250\t//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n251\t//\n252\t// # Returns\n253\t//\n254\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n255\t//\n256\t// # Example\n257\t//\n258\t//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n259\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n260\t//\n261\t//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n262\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n263\t//\n264\t//\t// Error message snippet\n265\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\n266\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n267\t\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n268\t\treturn vf\n269\t}\n270\t\n271\t// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n272\t//\n273\t// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n274\t// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n275\t// configuration objects.\n276\t//\n277\t// # Parameters\n278\t//\n279\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n280\t//\n281\t//     Common examples:\n282\t//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n283\t//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n284\t//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n285\t//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n286\t//\n287\t// # Returns\n288\t//\n289\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n290\t//\n291\t// # Example\n292\t//\n293\t//\t// Simple field name\n294\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n295\t//\n296\t//\t// Nested field notation\n297\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n298\t//\n299\t//\t// Array field notation\n300\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\n301\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n302\t\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n303\t\treturn vf\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n307\t//\n308\t// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n309\t// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n310\t// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n311\t//\n312\t// # Parameters\n313\t//\n314\t//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n315\t//\n316\t//     Common examples:\n317\t//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n318\t//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n319\t//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n320\t//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n321\t//\n322\t// # When to Use\n323\t//\n324\t// Use this method when:\n325\t//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n326\t//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n327\t//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n328\t//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n329\t//\n330\t// # Returns\n331\t//\n332\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n333\t//\n334\t// # Example\n335\t//\n336\t//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n337\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n338\t//\n339\t//\t// Format requirement\n340\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n341\t//\n342\t//\t// Content pattern description\n343\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\n344\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n345\t\tvf.patternDetails = details\n346\t\treturn vf\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n350\t//\n351\t// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n352\t// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n353\t// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n354\t//\n355\t// # Parameters\n356\t//\n357\t//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n358\t//\n359\t//     Common examples:\n360\t//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n361\t//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n362\t//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n363\t//\n364\t// # When to Use\n365\t//\n366\t// Use this method when:\n367\t//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n368\t//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n369\t//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n370\t//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n371\t//\n372\t// # Returns\n373\t//\n374\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n375\t//\n376\t// # Example\n377\t//\n378\t//\t// Status code range\n379\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n380\t//\n381\t//\t// Numeric range\n382\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n383\t//\n384\t//\t// Descriptive range\n385\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\n386\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n387\t\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n388\t\treturn vf\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n392\t//\n393\t// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n394\t// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n395\t// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n396\t//\n397\t// # Parameters\n398\t//\n399\t//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n400\t//\n401\t//     Common uses:\n402\t//     - List specific conditions that failed\n403\t//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n404\t//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n405\t//     - Document intermediate validation states\n406\t//\n407\t// # When to Use\n408\t//\n409\t// Use this method when:\n410\t//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n411\t//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n412\t//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n413\t//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n414\t//\n415\t// # Returns\n416\t//\n417\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n418\t//\n419\t// # Example\n420\t//\n421\t//\t// Multiple specific failures\n422\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n423\t//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n424\t//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n425\t//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n426\t//\t)\n427\t//\n428\t//\t// Detailed explanation\n429\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n430\t//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n431\t//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n432\t//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n433\t//\t)\n434\t//\n435\t//\t// Step-by-step validation\n436\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n437\t//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n438\t//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n439\t//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n440\t//\t)\n441\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n442\t\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n443\t\treturn vf\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n447\t//\n448\t// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n449\t// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n450\t// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n451\t//\n452\t// # Parameters\n453\t//\n454\t//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n455\t//\n456\t//     Best practices for suggestions:\n457\t//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n458\t//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n459\t//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n460\t//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n461\t//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n462\t//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n463\t//\n464\t// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n465\t//\n466\t// Use this method when:\n467\t//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n468\t//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n469\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n470\t//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n471\t//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n472\t//\n473\t// # Returns\n474\t//\n475\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n476\t//\n477\t// # Example\n478\t//\n479\t//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n480\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n481\t//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n482\t//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n483\t//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n484\t//\t)\n485\t//\n486\t//\t// Specific debugging steps\n487\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n488\t//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n489\t//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n490\t//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n491\t//\t)\n492\t//\n493\t//\t// Reference documentation\n494\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n495\t//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n496\t//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n497\t//\t)\n498\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n499\t\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n500\t\treturn vf\n501\t}\n502\t\n503\t// WithCategory sets the category for the validation error.\n504\t//\n505\t// This method specifies the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n506\t// If not set, the category will be auto-derived from the validation type.\n507\t//\n508\t// # Parameters\n509\t//\n510\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value (e.g., CategoryHTTP, CategoryContent, etc.)\n511\t//\n512\t// # Returns\n513\t//\n514\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n515\t//\n516\t// # Example\n517\t//\n518\t//\tformatter.WithCategory(validate.CategoryHTTP)\n519\t//\n520\t//\t// With chaining\n521\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n522\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n523\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n524\t//\t    WithCategory(validate.CategoryHTTP).\n525\t//\t    Format()\n526\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithCategory(category ErrorCategory) *ValidationFormatter {\n527\t\tvf.category = category\n528\t\treturn vf\n529\t}\n530\t\n531\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n532\t//\n533\t// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n534\t// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n535\t// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n536\t//\n537\t// # Behavior\n538\t//\n539\t// The Format() method:\n540\t//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n541\t//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n542\t//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n543\t//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n544\t//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n545\t//\n546\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n547\t//\n548\t// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n549\t//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n550\t//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n551\t//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n552\t//\n553\t// # Returns\n554\t//\n555\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n556\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n557\t// test assertions.\n558\t//\n559\t// # Example\n560\t//\n561\t//\t// Complete usage example\n562\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n563\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n564\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n565\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n566\t//\t    Format()\n567\t//\n568\t//\t// Error() method will produce:\n569\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n570\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n571\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n572\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n573\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n574\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n575\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n576\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n577\t//\n578\t//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n579\t//\treturn err\n580\t//\n581\t//\t// Or formatted for logging\n582\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n583\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n584\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n585\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n586\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n587\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Generate a concise message from validation parts\n591\t\tmessage := generateMessageFromParts(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n592\t\n593\t\t// Determine category - use explicitly set category or auto-derive from error type\n594\t\tcategory := vf.category\n595\t\tif category == \"\" {\n596\t\t\tcategory = GetCategoryForErrorType(vf.validationType)\n597\t\t}\n598\t\n599\t\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n600\t\treturn ValidationError{\n601\t\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n602\t\t\tMessage:           message,\n603\t\t\tCategory:          category,\n604\t\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n605\t\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n606\t\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n607\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n608\t\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n609\t\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n610\t\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n611\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n612\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n613\t\t}\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t// =============================================================================\n617\t// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n618\t// =============================================================================\n619\t//\n620\t// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n621\t// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n622\t//\n623\t// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n624\t//\n625\t// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n626\t//\n627\t// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n628\t//\n629\t// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n630\t//\n631\t//\t// Custom header validation\n632\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n633\t//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n634\t//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n635\t//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n636\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n637\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n638\t//\t    Format()\n639\t//\n640\t// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n641\t//\n642\t// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n643\t// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n644\t//\n645\t//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n646\t//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n647\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n648\t//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n649\t//\t        WithActual(actual).\n650\t//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n651\t//\t        WithContext(context).\n652\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n653\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n654\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n655\t//\t        ).\n656\t//\t        Format()\n657\t//\t}\n658\t//\n659\t// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n660\t//\n661\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n662\t//\n663\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n664\t//\t    \"response_time\",\n665\t//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n666\t//\t    \"250ms\",\n667\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n668\t//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n669\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n670\t//\t)\n671\t//\n672\t// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n673\t//\n674\t// When creating custom validation types:\n675\t//\n676\t// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n677\t//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n678\t//\n679\t// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n680\t//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n681\t//\n682\t// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n683\t//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n684\t//\n685\t// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n686\t//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n687\t//\n688\t// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n689\t//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n690\t//\n691\t// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n692\t//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n693\t//\n694\t// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n695\t//\n696\t// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n697\t// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n698\t// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n699\t// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n700\t//\n701\t// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n702\t//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n703\t//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n704\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n705\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n706\t//\n707\t// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n708\t//\n709\t// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n710\t//\n711\t//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n712\t//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n713\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n714\t//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n715\t//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n716\t//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n717\t//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n718\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n719\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n720\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n721\t//\t        ).\n722\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n723\t//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n724\t//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n725\t//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n726\t//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n727\t//\t        ).\n728\t//\t        Format()\n729\t//\t}\n730\t//\n731\t//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n732\t//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n733\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n734\t//\n735\t// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n736\t// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n737\t\n738\t// =============================================================================\n739\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n740\t// =============================================================================\n741\t\n742\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n743\t//\n744\t// Parameters:\n745\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n746\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n747\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n748\t//\n749\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n750\t//\n751\t// Example usage:\n752\t//\n753\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n754\t//\t// Output:\n755\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n756\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n757\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n758\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n759\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n760\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n761\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n762\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n763\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n764\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n765\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n766\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n767\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n768\t\t\tFormat()\n769\t}\n770\t\n771\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n772\t//\n773\t// Parameters:\n774\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n775\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n776\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n777\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n778\t//\n779\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n780\t//\n781\t// Example usage:\n782\t//\n783\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n784\t//\t// Output:\n785\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n786\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n787\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n788\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n789\t//\t//   Field:    error\n790\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n791\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n792\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n793\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n794\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n795\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n796\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n797\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n798\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n799\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n800\t\t\tFormat()\n801\t}\n802\t\n803\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n804\t//\n805\t// Parameters:\n806\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n807\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n808\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n809\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n810\t//\n811\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n812\t//\n813\t// Example usage:\n814\t//\n815\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\", \"status_code\")\n816\t//\t// Output:\n817\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n818\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n819\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n820\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n821\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n822\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n823\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n824\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n825\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n826\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n827\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n828\t\n829\t\t// Build range info string\n830\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n831\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n832\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n833\t\t}\n834\t\n835\t\tdetails := []string{}\n836\t\tif err != nil {\n837\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n838\t\t} else {\n839\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n840\t\t}\n841\t\n842\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n843\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n844\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n845\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n846\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n847\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n848\t\n849\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n850\t\tif err == nil {\n851\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n852\t\t}\n853\t\n854\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n855\t}\n856\t\n857\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n858\t//\n859\t// Parameters:\n860\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n861\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n862\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n863\t//\n864\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n865\t//\n866\t// Example usage:\n867\t//\n868\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n869\t//\t// Output:\n870\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n871\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n872\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n873\t//   Context:  API response\n874\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n875\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n876\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n877\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n878\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n879\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n880\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n881\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n882\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n883\t\t\tFormat()\n884\t}\n885\t\n886\t// FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError creates a validation error for min/max range validation failures.\n887\t//\n888\t// This function is used when validating status codes (or other numeric values) against\n889\t// explicit minimum and maximum boundaries. It provides enhanced error messages showing\n890\t// the exact range that was expected and the actual value that was received.\n891\t//\n892\t// Parameters:\n893\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value (inclusive)\n894\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value (inclusive)\n895\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that was received\n896\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation (e.g., \"status code validation\")\n897\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n898\t//\n899\t// Returns a ValidationError with range information and appropriate suggestions.\n900\t//\n901\t// Example usage:\n902\t//\n903\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(200, 299, 404, \"success response check\", \"status_code\")\n904\t//\t// Output:\n905\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n906\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n907\t//\t//   Expected: 200-299 (Success)\n908\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n909\t//\t//   Range:    200-299 (Success)\n910\t//\t//   Context:  success response check\n911\t//\t//   Validation Details:\n912\t//\t//     - Status code 404 is outside valid range 200-299\n913\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n914\t//\t//     - Check if the endpoint URL is correct\n915\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n916\t//\t//     - Ensure authentication credentials are valid\n917\tfunc FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minValue, maxValue, actualValue int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n918\t\t// Get range description\n919\t\tdesc := getRangeDescriptionForValues(minValue, maxValue)\n920\t\n921\t\t// Build range info string\n922\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)\n923\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n924\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", minValue, maxValue, desc)\n925\t\t}\n926\t\n927\t\tdetails := []string{\n928\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside valid range %d-%d\", actualValue, minValue, maxValue),\n929\t\t}\n930\t\n931\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n932\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)).\n933\t\t\tWithActual(actualValue).\n934\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n935\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n936\t\t\tWithRangeInfo(rangeInfo).\n937\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...).\n938\t\t\tFormat()\n939\t}\n940\t\n941\t// getRangeDescriptionForValues returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n942\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges.\n943\tfunc getRangeDescriptionForValues(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n944\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n945\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n946\t\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n947\t\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n948\t\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n949\t\t\t\"100-199\": \"Informational\",\n950\t\t}\n951\t\n952\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n953\t\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n954\t\t\treturn desc\n955\t\t}\n956\t\n957\t\treturn \"\"\n958\t\n959\t}\n960\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n961\t//\n962\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n963\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n964\t// your specific requirements.\n965\t//\n966\t// Parameters:\n967\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n968\t//   - expected: The expected value\n969\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n970\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n971\t//\n972\t// Example usage:\n973\t//\n974\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n975\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n976\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n977\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n978\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n979\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n980\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n981\t//\t)\n982\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n983\t\tvalidationType string,\n984\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n985\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n986\t) ValidationError {\n987\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n988\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n989\t\t\topt(config)\n990\t\t}\n991\t\n992\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n993\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n994\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n995\t\n996\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n997\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n998\t\t}\n999\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n1000\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n1001\t\t}\n1002\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n1003\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n1004\t\t}\n1005\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n1006\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n1007\t\t}\n1008\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n1009\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n1010\t\t}\n1011\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n1012\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n1013\t\t}\n1014\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n1015\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n1016\t\t}\n1017\t\n1018\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n1019\t}\n1020\t\n1021\t// =============================================================================\n1022\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n1023\t// =============================================================================\n1024\t\n1025\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n1026\ttype FormatConfig string\n1032\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n1033\t\tSuggestions        []string\n1034\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n1035\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n1036\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n1037\t\tStatusCode         int\n1038\t\tTimeout            int\n1039\t\tSecurityContext    string\n1040\t}\n1041\t\n1042\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a fieldName\n1063\t\t}\n1064\t}\n1065\t\n1066\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n1067\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n1068\t\treturn WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n1123\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1124\t\t\tc.CategoryHint = category\n1125\t\t}\n1126\t}\n1127\t\n1128\t// WithStatusCode creates a {\n1175\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n1176\t\t}\n1177\t}\n1178\t\n1179\t\n1180\t// =============================================================================\n1181\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1182\t// =============================================================================\n1183\t\n1184\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1185\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1186\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1187\t\tif err != nil {\n1188\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1189\t\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1192\t\tif err != nil {\n1193\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1194\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1195\t\t}\n1196\t\n1197\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1198\t}\n1199\t\n1200\t// =============================================================================\n1201\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1202\t// =============================================================================\n1203\t\n1204\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1205\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1206\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1207\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1208\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1209\t}\n1210\t\n1211\t// global tracker instance\n1212\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1213\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1214\t}\n1215\t\n1216\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1217\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1218\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1219\t//\n1220\t// Parameters:\n1221\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1222\t//\n1223\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1224\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1225\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1226\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1227\t\n1228\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1229\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1230\t}\n1231\t\n1232\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1233\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1234\t// configuration issues.\n1235\t//\n1236\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1237\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1238\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1239\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1240\t\n1241\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1242\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1243\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1244\t\t}\n1245\t\treturn result\n1246\t}\n1247\t\n1248\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1249\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1250\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1251\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1252\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1253\t\n1254\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1255\t}\n1256\t\n1257\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1258\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1259\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1260\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1261\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1262\t\n1263\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1264\t}\n1265\t\n1266\t// =============================================================================\n1267\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1268\t// =============================================================================\n1269\t\n1270\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1271\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1272\t//\n1273\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1274\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1275\t//\n1276\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1277\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1278\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1279\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1280\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1281\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1282\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1283\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1284\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1285\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1286\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1287\t//\n1288\t// Parameters:\n1289\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1290\t//   - message: The error message content\n1291\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1292\t//\n1293\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1294\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1295\t//\n1296\t// Example usage:\n1297\t//\n1298\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1299\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1300\t//\n1301\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1302\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1303\t//\n1304\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1305\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1306\t//\n1307\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1308\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1309\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1310\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1311\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1312\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1313\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1314\t\n1315\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1316\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1317\t\n1318\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1319\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1320\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1321\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1322\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1323\t\t\t} else {\n1324\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1325\t\t\t}\n1326\t\t}\n1327\t\n1328\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1329\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1330\t}\n1331\t\n1332\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1333\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1334\t//\n1335\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1336\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1337\t//\n1338\t// Error Type Validation:\n1339\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1340\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1341\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1342\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1343\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1344\t//\n1345\t// Parameters:\n1346\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1347\t//   - message: The error message content\n1348\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1349\t//\n1350\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1351\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1352\t//\n1353\t// Example usage:\n1354\t//\n1355\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1356\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1357\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1358\t//\n1359\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1360\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1361\t//\n1362\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1363\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1364\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1365\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1366\t//\n1367\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1368\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1369\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1370\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1371\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1372\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1373\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1374\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1375\t\t}\n1376\t\n1377\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1378\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1379\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1380\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1381\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1382\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1383\t\n1384\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1385\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1386\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1387\t\t}\n1388\t\n1389\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1390\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1391\t\n1392\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1393\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1394\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1395\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1396\t\n1397\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1398\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1399\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1400\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1401\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1402\t\t}\n1403\t\n1404\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1405\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1406\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1407\t\n1408\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1409\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1410\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1411\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1412\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1413\t\t\t} else {\n1414\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1415\t\t\t}\n1416\t\t}\n1417\t\n1418\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1419\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1420\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1421\t\t}\n1422\t\n1423\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1424\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1425\t}\n1426\t\n1427\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1428\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1429\t//\n1430\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1431\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1432\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1433\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1434\t}\n1435\t\n1436\t// =============================================================================\n1437\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1438\t// =============================================================================\n1439\t\n1440\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1441\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1442\t\n1443\tconst (\n1444\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1445\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1446\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1447\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1448\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1449\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1450\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1451\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1452\t)\n1453\t\n1454\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1455\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1456\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1457\t\tprefix     string\n1458\t}\n1459\t\n1460\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1461\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1462\t\n1463\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1464\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1465\t//\n1466\t// Example usage:\n1467\t//\n1468\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1469\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1470\t//\n1471\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1472\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1473\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1474\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1475\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1476\t\t}\n1477\t}\n1478\t\n1479\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1480\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1481\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1482\t//\n1483\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1484\t//\n1485\t// Example usage:\n1486\t//\n1487\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1488\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1489\t//\n1490\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1491\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1492\t//\n1493\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1494\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1495\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1496\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1497\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1498\t\t}\n1499\t}\n1500\t\n1501\t// =============================================================================\n1502\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1503\t// =============================================================================\n1504\t\n1505\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1506\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1507\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1508\t//\n1509\t// Parameters:\n1510\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1511\t//\n1512\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1513\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1514\t//\n1515\t// Example usage:\n1516\t//\n1517\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1518\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1519\t//\n1520\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1521\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1522\t//\n1523\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1524\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1525\t//\n1526\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1527\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1528\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1529\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1530\t\n1531\t\t// Handle empty path\n1532\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1533\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1534\t\t}\n1535\t\n1536\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1537\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1538\t}\n1539\t\n1540\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1541\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1542\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1543\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1544\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1545\t\t}\n1546\t\n1547\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1548\t\tvar result []string\n1549\t\n1550\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1551\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1552\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1553\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1554\t\t\t}\n1555\t\n1556\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1557\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1558\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1559\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1560\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1561\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1562\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1563\t\t\t\t} else {\n1564\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1565\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1566\t\t\t\t}\n1567\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1568\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1569\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1570\t\t\t} else {\n1571\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1572\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1573\t\t\t}\n1574\t\t}\n1575\t\n1576\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1577\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1578\t\t}\n1579\t\n1580\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1581\t}\n1582\t\n1583\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1584\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1585\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1586\t\t\treturn false\n1587\t\t}\n1588\t\n1589\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1590\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1591\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1592\t\t\t\treturn false\n1593\t\t\t}\n1594\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1595\t\t}\n1596\t\n1597\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1598\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1599\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1600\t\t\t\treturn false\n1601\t\t\t}\n1602\t\t}\n1603\t\n1604\t\treturn true\n1605\t}\n1606\t\n1607\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1608\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1609\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1610\t}\n1611\t\n1612\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1613\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1614\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1615\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1616\t//\n1617\t// Parameters:\n1618\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1619\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1620\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1621\t//\n1622\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1623\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1624\t//\n1625\t// Example usage:\n1626\t//\n1627\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1628\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1629\t//\n1630\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1631\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1632\t//\n1633\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1634\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1635\t//\n1636\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1637\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1638\t//\n1639\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1640\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1641\t//\n1642\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1643\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1644\t//\n1645\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1646\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1647\t//\n1648\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1649\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1650\t//\n1651\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1652\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1653\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1654\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1655\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1656\t\n1657\t\t// Apply options\n1658\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1659\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1660\t\t\topt(config)\n1661\t\t}\n1662\t\n1663\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1664\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1665\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1666\t\t}\n1667\t\n1668\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1669\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1670\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1671\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1672\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1673\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1674\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1675\t\t\t\t}\n1676\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1677\t\t\t}\n1678\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1679\t\t}\n1680\t\n1681\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1682\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1683\t\n1684\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1685\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1686\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1687\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1688\t\t\t}\n1689\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1690\t\t}\n1691\t\n1692\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1693\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1694\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1695\t\t}\n1696\t\n1697\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1698\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1699\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1700\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1701\t\n1702\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1703\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1704\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1705\t\t\t}\n1706\t\n1707\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1708\t\t}\n1709\t\n1710\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1711\t}\n1712\t\n1713\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1714\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1715\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1716\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1717\t\t\treturn path\n1718\t\t}\n1719\t\n1720\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1721\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1722\t\tinBrackets := false\n1723\t\n1724\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1725\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1726\t\n1727\t\t\tswitch {\n1728\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1729\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1730\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1731\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1732\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1733\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1734\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1735\t\t\t\t}\n1736\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1737\t\n1738\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1739\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1740\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1741\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1742\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1743\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1744\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1745\t\t\t\t}\n1746\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1747\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1748\t\n1749\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1750\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1751\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1752\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1753\t\n1754\t\t\tdefault:\n1755\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1756\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1757\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1758\t\t\t\t} else {\n1759\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1760\t\t\t\t}\n1761\t\t\t}\n1762\t\t}\n1763\t\n1764\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1765\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1766\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1767\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1768\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1769\t\t}\n1770\t\n1771\t\treturn result.String()\n1772\t}\n1773\t\n1774\t// =============================================================================\n1775\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1776\t// =============================================================================\n1777\t\n1778\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1779\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1780\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1781\t//\n1782\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1783\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1784\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1785\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1786\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1787\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1788\t//\n1789\t// Parameters:\n1790\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1791\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1792\t//   - message: The error message content\n1793\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1794\t//\n1795\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1796\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1797\t//\n1798\t// Example usage:\n1799\t//\n1800\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1801\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1802\t//\n1803\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1804\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1805\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1806\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1807\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1808\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1809\t\t}\n1810\t\n1811\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1812\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1813\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1814\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1815\t\n1816\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1817\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1818\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1819\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1820\t\t\t} else {\n1821\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1822\t\t\t}\n1823\t\t}\n1824\t\n1825\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1826\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1827\t\n1828\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1829\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1830\t\n1831\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1832\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1833\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1834\t\n1835\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1836\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1837\t\n1838\t\t// Add field name if present\n1839\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1840\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1841\t\t}\n1842\t\n1843\t\t// Add message\n1844\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1845\t\n1846\t\treturn builder.String()\n1847\t}\n1848\t\n1849\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1850\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1851\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1852\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1853\t\n1854\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1855\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1856\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1857\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1858\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1859\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1860\t\t}\n1861\t\n1862\t\treturn \"\"\n1863\t}\n1864\t\n1865\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1866\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1867\t\tswitch severity {\n1868\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1869\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1870\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1871\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1872\t\tcase \"UNK\"\n1880\t\t}\n1881\t}\n1882\t\n1883\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1884\t// both category and severity awareness. FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1915\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1916\t\tmessage string,\n1917\t\tfieldName string,\n1918\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1919\t) string {\n1920\t\t// Get default config.CategoryHint\n1936\t\t}\n1937\t\n1938\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1939\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1940\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1941\t\n1942\t\t// {\n1947\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1948\t\t\t}\n1949\t\t}\n1950\t\n1951\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1952\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1953\t\n1954\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n1955\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n1956\t\n1957\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1958\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1959\t\n1960\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1961\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1962\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1963\t\n1964\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n1965\t\tif category != brackets\n1971\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n1972\t\n1973\t\t// Add field name if present\n1974\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1975\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1976\t\t}\n1977\t\n1978\t\t// Add message\n1979\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1980\t\n1981\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1982\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1983\t\n1984\t\treturn builder.String()\n1985\t}\n1986\t\n1987\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1988\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1989\t\tswitch category {\n1990\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1991\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1992\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1993\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1994\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1995\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1996\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1997\t\t\treturn \"\"\n2004\t\t}\n2005\t}\n2006\t\n2007\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n2008\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n2009\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n2010\t\tif config == nil {\n2011\t\t\treturn \"\"\n2012\t\t}\n2013\t\n2014\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n2015\t\n2016\t\tswitch =============================================================================\n2038\t// STREAMLINED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING HELPER\n2039\t// =============================================================================\n2040\t\n2041\t// FormatValidationErrorHelper is a streamlined helper function for formatting validation errors consistently.\n2042\t// This function provides a simple, unified interface for creating validation errors with optional\n2043\t// context, expected/actual values, and custom suggestions.\n2044\t//\n2045\t// This helper is designed to be the primary entry point for validation error formatting,\n2046\t// offering a balance between simplicity and flexibility. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2059\t//\n2060\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization (variadic parameter)\n2061\t//     Use validation helper options like WithContext(), WithSuggestions(), etc.\n2062\t//\n2063\t// # Returns\n2064\t//\n2065\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields that implements\n2066\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or test assertions.\n2067\t//\n2068\t// # Features\n2069\t//\n2070\t//   - **Unified Interface**: Single function for all validation error types\n2071\t//   - **Type Safety**: Works with any value type for expected/actual\n2072\t//   - **Auto-generated Suggestions**: Smart suggestions based on error type and values\n2073\t//   - **Custom Suggestions**: Override with domain-specific suggestions\n2074\t//   - **Flexible Context**: Add contextual information for debugging\n2075\t//   - **Optional Fields**: Only provide what you need for your scenario\n2076\t//\n2077\t// # Common Usage Patterns\n2078\t//\n2079\t// Basic usage with just error type and values:\n2080\t//\n2081\t//\terr := validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n)\n\n// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n//\n// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n//\n// # Design Pattern\n//\n// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n//   - Type-safe error construction\n//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n//\n// # Common Usage Pattern\n//\n// The typical workflow is:\n//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// With custom suggestions\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n// # Extending for New Validation Types\n//\n// To add support for a new validation type:\n//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n//  4.
