{\n75\t//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n76\t//\t}\n77\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n78\t\tif resp == nil {\n79\t\t\treturn false\n80\t\t}\n81\t\n82\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a server error (5xx).\n86\t//\n87\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 500-599 range, false otherwise.\n88\t//\n89\t// Example usage:\n90\t//\n91\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(response) {\n92\t//\t    // Handle 5xx server error (internal server error, bad gateway, etc.)\n93\t//\t}\n94\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n95\t\tif resp == nil {\n96\t\t\treturn false\n97\t\t}\n98\t\n99\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t// ContentTypeIsValid checks if a response's Content-Type header matches the expected pattern.\n103\t// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\n104\t// For example, \"application/json\" will match \"application/json; charset=utf-8\".\n105\t//\n106\t// Parameters:\n107\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n108\t//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n109\t//\n110\t// Returns true if the response Content-Type matches the expected pattern, false otherwise.\n111\t// Returns false if the response is nil or has no Content-Type header.\n112\t//\n113\t// Example usage:\n114\t//\n115\t//\tif ContentTypeIsValid(response, \"application/json\") {\n116\t//\t    // Handle JSON response\n117\t//\t}\n118\tfunc ContentTypeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected string) bool {\n119\t\tif resp == nil {\n120\t\t\treturn false\n121\t\t}\n122\t\n123\t\tactual := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n124\t\tif actual == \"\" {\n125\t\t\treturn false\n126\t\t}\n127\t\n128\t\t// Parse both content-type strings to extract base media types\n129\t\tactualMediaType := parseContentType(actual)\n130\t\texpectedMediaType := parseContentType(expected)\n131\t\n132\t\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\t// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\n136\t// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\n137\t// For example, \"application/json; charset=utf-8\" becomes \"application/json\".\n138\tfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\n139\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n140\t\t\treturn \"\"\n141\t\t}\n142\t\n143\t\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\n144\t\t// The first part before any semicolon is the base media type\n145\t\tfor i, c := range contentType {\n146\t\t\tif c == ';' {\n147\t\t\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType[:i])\n148\t\t\t}\n149\t\t}\n150\t\n151\t\t// No parameters found, return the whole string trimmed\n152\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType)\n153\t}\n154\t\n155\t// trimSpace removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.\n156\tfunc trimSpace(s string) string {\n157\t\tstart := 0\n158\t\tend := len(s)\n159\t\n160\t\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\t' || s[start] == '\\n' || s[start] == '\\r') {\n161\t\t\tstart++\n162\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\tfor end > start && (s[end-1] == ' ' || s[end-1] == '\\t' || s[end-1] == '\\n' || s[end-1] == '\\r') {\n165\t\t\tend--\n166\t\t}\n167\t\n168\t\treturn s[start:end]\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// ErrorResponseFieldNames specifies custom field names to check when validating error response structures.\n172\t// If nil, the default field names \"error\" and \"message\" are checked.\n173\ttype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n174\t\t// PrimaryFieldName is the main error field to check (e.g., \"error\", \"detail\", \"error_description\")\n175\t\tPrimaryFieldName string\n176\t\t// SecondaryFieldName is an optional secondary field to check (e.g., \"message\", \"description\")\n177\t\tSecondaryFieldName string\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t// DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames returns the default field names used for error response validation.\n181\t// This checks for both \"error\" and \"message\" fields.\n182\tfunc DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames() *ErrorResponseFieldNames {\n183\t\treturn &ErrorResponseFieldNames{\n184\t\t\tPrimaryFieldName:   \"error\",\n185\t\t\tSecondaryFieldName: \"message\",\n186\t\t}\n187\t}\n188\t\n189\t// ErrorResponseStructureIsValid validates whether a response object contains a valid error response structure.\n190\t// It checks for the presence of common error fields and returns whether the structure is valid.\n191\t//\n192\t// The response parameter can be:\n193\t//   - map[string]interface{}: A parsed JSON object representing the response body\n194\t//   - Any type that can be inspected for error fields using reflection\n195\t//\n196\t// The fieldNames parameter allows customization of which fields to check:\n197\t//   - If nil, checks for default fields \"error\" and \"message\"\n198\t//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\n199\t//\n200\t// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\n201\t// false otherwise.\n202\t//\n203\t// Example usage:\n204\t//\n205\t//\t// Using default field names (checks for \"error\" and \"message\")\n206\t//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"Invalid input\"}\n207\t//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\n208\t//\n209\t//\t// Using custom field names\n210\t//\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \"detail\", SecondaryFieldName: \"description\"}\n211\t//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"detail\": \"Resource not found\"}\n212\t//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\n213\tfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\n214\t\tif response == nil {\n215\t\t\treturn false\n216\t\t}\n217\t\n218\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n219\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n220\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\n224\t\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\n225\t\tif !ok {\n226\t\t\treturn false\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\t// Check primary field\n230\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n231\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n232\t\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n233\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n234\t\t\t\t}\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t}\n237\t\n238\t\t// Check secondary field\n239\t\tif fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n240\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n241\t\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n242\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n243\t\t\t\t}\n244\t\t\t}\n245\t\t}\n246\t\n247\t\treturn false\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\n251\t// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\n252\t// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\n253\tfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\n254\t\tif value == nil {\n255\t\t\treturn false\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n259\t\tcase string:\n260\t\t\treturn v != \"\"\n261\t\tcase int:\n262\t\t\treturn v != 0\n263\t\tcase int8:\n264\t\t\treturn v != 0\n265\t\tcase int16:\n266\t\t\treturn v != 0\n267\t\tcase int32:\n268\t\t\treturn v != 0\n269\t\tcase int64:\n270\t\t\treturn v != 0\n271\t\tcase uint:\n272\t\t\treturn v != 0\n273\t\tcase uint8:\n274\t\t\treturn v != 0\n275\t\tcase uint16:\n276\t\t\treturn v != 0\n277\t\tcase uint32:\n278\t\t\treturn v != 0\n279\t\tcase uint64:\n280\t\t\treturn v != 0\n281\t\tcase float32:\n282\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n283\t\tcase float64:\n284\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n285\t\tcase bool:\n286\t\t\treturn v\n287\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n288\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n289\t\tcase []interface{}:\n290\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n291\t\tdefault:\n292\t\t\treturn true\n293\t\t}\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\n297\ttype CORSConfig struct {\n298\t\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\n299\t\t// Use \"*\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \"https://example.com\".\n300\t\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\n301\t\tAllowOrigin string\n302\t\n303\t\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\n304\t\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\n305\t\tAllowMethods string\n306\t\n307\t\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\n308\t\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\n309\t\tAllowHeaders string\n310\t\n311\t\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\n312\t\t// If true, expects \"true\"; if false, expects \"true\" or header not present.\n313\t\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\n314\t\tAllowCredentials bool\n315\t\n316\t\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\n317\t\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\n318\t\tExposeHeaders string\n319\t\n320\t\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\n321\t\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n322\t\tMaxAge string\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n326\t// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n327\t//\n328\t// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n329\t// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n330\t//\n331\t// Parameters:\n332\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n333\t//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n334\t//\n335\t// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n336\t// Returns false if the response is nil.\n337\t//\n338\t// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n339\t// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n340\t//\n341\t// Example usage:\n342\t//\n343\t//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n344\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n345\t//\t    // CORS headers are present\n346\t//\t}\n347\t//\n348\t//\t// Validate specific origin\n349\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n350\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n351\t//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n352\t//\t}\n353\t//\n354\t//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n355\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n356\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n357\t//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n358\t//\t}\n359\t//\n360\t//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n361\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n362\t//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n363\t//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n364\t//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n365\t//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n366\t//\t}\n367\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n368\t//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n369\t//\t}\n370\tfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n371\t\tif resp == nil {\n372\t\t\treturn false\n373\t\t}\n374\t\n375\t\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n376\t\tif config == nil {\n377\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n378\t\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n379\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n382\t\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n383\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n384\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n385\t\t\t\treturn false\n386\t\t\t}\n387\t\n388\t\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n389\t\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n390\t\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n391\t\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n392\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n393\t\t\t\t}\n394\t\t\t} else {\n395\t\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n396\t\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n397\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n398\t\t\t\t}\n399\t\t\t}\n400\t\t}\n401\t\n402\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n403\t\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n404\t\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n405\t\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n406\t\t\t\treturn false\n407\t\t\t}\n408\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n411\t\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n412\t\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n413\t\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n414\t\t\t\treturn false\n415\t\t\t}\n416\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n419\t\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n420\t\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n421\t\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n422\t\t\t\treturn false\n423\t\t\t}\n424\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n427\t\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n428\t\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n429\t\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n430\t\t\t\treturn false\n431\t\t\t}\n432\t\t}\n433\t\n434\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n435\t\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n436\t\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n437\t\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n438\t\t\t\treturn false\n439\t\t\t}\n440\t\t}\n441\t\n442\t\treturn true\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// =============================================================================\n446\t// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n447\t// =============================================================================\n448\t\n449\t// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\n450\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n451\t\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n452\t\tPattern string\n453\t\n454\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n455\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n456\t\n457\t\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n458\t\tMatchAny bool\n459\t\n460\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n461\t\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n462\t\tFieldNames []string\n463\t}\n464\t\n465\t// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n466\t//\n467\t// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n468\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n469\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n470\t//\n471\t// Parameters:\n472\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n473\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n474\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n475\t//\n476\t// Returns:\n477\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n478\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n479\t//\n480\t// Example usage:\n481\t//\n482\t//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n483\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n484\t//\n485\t//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n486\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\n487\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n488\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n489\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\tif pattern == \"\" {\n493\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\t// Parse JSON body\n497\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n498\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n499\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n500\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n503\t\tregexPattern := pattern\n504\t\tif caseInsensitive {\n505\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n506\t\t}\n507\t\n508\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n509\t\tif err != nil {\n510\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n511\t\t}\n512\t\n513\t\t// Check default error fields\n514\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n515\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n516\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n517\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n518\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n519\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n520\t\t\t\t\t}\n521\t\t\t\t}\n522\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n523\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n524\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n525\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n526\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n527\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n528\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n530\t\t\t\t\t}\n531\t\t\t\t}\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\treturn false, nil\n536\t}\n537\t\n538\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n539\t//\n540\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n541\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n542\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n543\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n544\t//\n545\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n546\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n547\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n548\t//\n549\t// Parameters:\n550\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n551\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n552\t//\n553\t// Returns:\n554\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n555\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n556\t//\n557\t// Example usage:\n558\t//\n559\t//\t// Regex pattern matching\n560\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n561\t//\tif err != nil {\n562\t//\t    // Pattern not found\n563\t//\t}\n564\t//\n565\t//\t// Simple substring matching\n566\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n567\t//\tif err != nil {\n568\t//\t    // Substring not found\n569\t//\t}\n570\t//\n571\t//\t// Common error patterns\n572\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n573\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n574\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n575\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n576\t//\n577\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n578\t//\n579\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n580\t//\n581\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n582\t//\n583\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n584\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n585\t//\n586\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n587\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n588\t//\n589\t// # Authentication Errors\n590\t//\n591\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n592\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n593\t//\n594\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n595\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n596\t//\n597\t// # Authorization Errors\n598\t//\n599\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n600\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n601\t//\n602\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n603\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n604\t//\n605\t// # Validation Errors\n606\t//\n607\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n608\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"validation.*failed\")  // regex match\n609\t//\n610\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\"}`)\n611\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*input\")  // regex match\n612\t//\n613\t// # Resource Errors\n614\t//\n615\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Resource not found\"}`)\n616\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not found\")  // substring match\n617\t//\n618\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"User does not exist\"}`)\n619\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"does not exist\")  // substring match\n620\t//\n621\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\n622\t//\n623\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}`)\n624\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"rate.*limit\")  // regex match\n625\t//\n626\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Too many requests, please try again later\"}`)\n627\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"too many requests\")  // substring match\n628\t//\n629\t// # Timeout Errors\n630\t//\n631\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Request timeout\"}`)\n632\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n633\t//\n634\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\"}`)\n635\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n636\t//\n637\t// # Server Errors\n638\t//\n639\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}`)\n640\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"internal.*error\")  // regex match\n641\t//\n642\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Service temporarily unavailable\"}`)\n643\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"unavailable\")  // substring match\n644\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\n645\t\t// Validate inputs\n646\t\tif len(response) == 0 {\n647\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n648\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"non-empty response\").\n649\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n650\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"cannot validate against empty response\").\n651\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n652\t\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n653\t\t\t\t\t\"Response body is empty\",\n654\t\t\t\t\t\"Cannot validate error message pattern against empty response\",\n655\t\t\t\t).\n656\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n657\t\t\t\t\t\"Response body is empty\",\n658\t\t\t\t\t\"Cannot validate error message pattern against empty response\",\n659\t\t\t\t).\n660\t\t\t\tFormat()\n661\t\t}\n662\t\n663\t\tif expectedPattern == \"\" {\n664\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n665\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"non-empty pattern\").\n666\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n667\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"pattern cannot be empty\").\n668\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n669\t\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n670\t\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern cannot be empty\",\n671\t\t\t\t\t\"Provide a pattern to match against error messages\",\n672\t\t\t\t).\n673\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n674\t\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern cannot be empty\",\n675\t\t\t\t\t\"Provide a pattern to match against error messages\",\n676\t\t\t\t).\n677\t\t\t\tFormat()\n678\t\t}\n679\t\n680\t\t// Parse JSON body\n681\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n682\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\n683\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n684\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"valid JSON\").\n685\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n686\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"failed to parse response body\").\n687\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n688\t\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err)).\n689\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n690\t\t\t\t\t\"Failed to parse response body\",\n691\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err),\n692\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure response contains valid JSON\",\n693\t\t\t\t).\n694\t\t\t\tFormat()\n695\t\t}\n696\t\n697\t\t// Search for error messages in common fields\n698\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n699\t\ttype foundMessage struct {\n700\t\t\tmessage   string\n701\t\t\tfieldName string\n702\t\t}\n703\t\tvar foundMessages []foundMessage\n704\t\n705\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n706\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n707\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n708\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, foundMessage{\n709\t\t\t\t\t\tmessage:   strValue,\n710\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName: field,\n711\t\t\t\t\t})\n712\t\t\t\t}\n713\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n714\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n715\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n716\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n717\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, foundMessage{\n718\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmessage:   strValue,\n719\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName: field + \".message\",\n720\t\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n721\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n722\t\t\t\t\t}\n723\t\t\t\t}\n724\t\t\t}\n725\t\t}\n726\t\n727\t\t// If no error messages found, return detailed error with response snippet\n728\t\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\n729\t\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n730\t\t\tresponseSize := len(response)\n731\t\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n732\t\t\t\tWithExpected(\"error message field\").\n733\t\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n734\t\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"no error message found in response\").\n735\t\t\t\tWithResponseSnippet(snippet).\n736\t\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n737\t\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n738\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n739\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Response size: %d bytes\", responseSize),\n740\t\t\t\t).\n741\t\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n742\t\t\t\t\t\"No error message found in response body\",\n743\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n744\t\t\t\t\t\"Response may not contain standard error fields\",\n745\t\t\t\t).\n746\t\t\t\tFormat()\n747\t\t}\n748\t\n749\t\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\n750\t\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\n751\t\n752\t\t// Check each found message for pattern match\n753\t\tfor _, foundMsg := range foundMessages {\n754\t\t\tvar matched bool\n755\t\n756\t\t\tif isRegex {\n757\t\t\t\t// Try regex matching\n758\t\t\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\n759\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n760\t\t\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n761\t\t\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n762\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"valid regex pattern '%s'\", expectedPattern),\n763\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid regex '%s'\", expectedPattern),\n764\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n765\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n766\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMsg.fieldName,\n767\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n768\t\t\t\t\t\tnil,\n769\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern type: regex (contains metacharacters), Parse error: %v\", err),\n770\t\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n771\t\t\t\t\t\t[]string{\n772\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid regex pattern: %s\", expectedPattern),\n773\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Regex compilation error: %v\", err),\n774\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Check regex syntax and escape special characters\",\n775\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n776\t\t\t\t\t)\n777\t\t\t\t}\n778\t\t\t\tmatched = re.MatchString(foundMsg.message)\n779\t\t\t} else {\n780\t\t\t\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\n781\t\t\t\tmatched = strings.Contains(foundMsg.message, expectedPattern)\n782\t\t\t}\n783\t\n784\t\t\tif matched {\n785\t\t\t\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\n786\t\t\t\treturn nil\n787\t\t\t}\n788\t\t}\n789\t\n790\t\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error with full details\n791\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n792\t\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\n793\t\n794\t\t// Build validation details including response size context\n795\t\tresponseSize := len(response)\n796\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n797\t\tif isRegex {\n798\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, \"Searched for regex pattern\")\n799\t\t} else {\n800\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, \"Searched for substring\")\n801\t\t}\n802\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected pattern: %s\", expectedPattern))\n803\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual message: \\\"%s\\\"\", firstMessage.message))\n804\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Field name: %s\", firstMessage.fieldName))\n805\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")))\n806\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Response size: %d bytes\", responseSize))\n807\t\n808\t\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\n809\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Found %d error message fields in response\", len(foundMessages)))\n810\t\t}\n811\t\n812\t\t// Generate pattern-specific suggestions\n813\t\tsuggestions := generatePatternMismatchSuggestions(expectedPattern, firstMessage.message, isRegex)\n814\t\n815\t\t// Build pattern details\n816\t\tvar patternDetails string\n817\t\tif isRegex {\n818\t\t\tpatternDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"regex pattern '%s' did not match\", expectedPattern)\n819\t\t} else {\n820\t\t\tpatternDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"substring '%s' did not match\", expectedPattern)\n821\t\t}\n822\t\n823\t\t// Use ValidationFormatter builder pattern for enhanced error formatting\n824\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n825\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n826\t\t\tWithActual(firstMessage.message).\n827\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(patternDetails).\n828\t\t\tWithResponseSnippet(snippet).\n829\t\t\tWithFieldName(firstMessage.fieldName).\n830\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n831\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(validationDetails...).\n832\t\t\tWithSuggestions(suggestions...).\n833\t\t\tFormat()\n834\t}\n835\t\n836\t// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\n837\t// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\n838\tfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\n839\t\tregexChars := []string{\".\", \"*\", \"+\", \"?\", \"^\", \"$\", \"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \"(\", \")\", \"|\", \"\\\\\"}\n840\t\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\n841\t\t\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\n842\t\t\t\treturn true\n843\t\t\t}\n844\t\t}\n845\t\treturn false\n846\t}\n847\t\n848\t// generatePatternMismatchSuggestions provides relevant suggestions based on pattern and actual message.\n849\t// This function analyzes the expected pattern and actual message to generate specific suggestions.\n850\tfunc generatePatternMismatchSuggestions(expectedPattern, actualMessage string, isRegex bool) []string {\n851\t\tvar suggestions []string\n852\t\n853\t\texpectedLower := strings.ToLower(expectedPattern)\n854\t\tactualLower := strings.ToLower(actualMessage)\n855\t\n856\t\t// Common pattern mismatch checks\n857\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"token\") && !strings.Contains(actualLower, \"token\") {\n858\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n859\t\t\t\t\"Pattern looks for 'token' but actual message doesn't contain it\",\n860\t\t\t\t\"Check if the error is about authentication/authorization rather than tokens\",\n861\t\t\t\t\"Consider expanding pattern to include 'auth' or 'access' related terms\",\n862\t\t\t)\n863\t\t}\n864\t\n865\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"invalid\") && !strings.Contains(actualLower, \"invalid\") {\n866\t\t\tif strings.Contains(actualLower, \"expired\") {\n867\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n868\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern expects 'invalid' but message contains 'expired'\",\n869\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using pattern: 'invalid.*expired|expired.*invalid' to match both cases\",\n870\t\t\t\t\t\"Or use separate patterns for different token states\",\n871\t\t\t\t)\n872\t\t\t}\n873\t\t}\n874\t\n875\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"not found\") && strings.Contains(actualLower, \"does not exist\") {\n876\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n877\t\t\t\t\"Pattern uses 'not found' but message uses 'does not exist'\",\n878\t\t\t\t\"Consider using pattern: 'not found|does not exist' to match both phrasings\",\n879\t\t\t\t\"Or use simpler pattern: 'not.*found|does.*not.*exist'\",\n880\t\t\t)\n881\t\t}\n882\t\n883\t\tif isRegex {\n884\t\t\t// Regex-specific suggestions\n885\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \".\") && !strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"\\\\\") {\n886\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n887\t\t\t\t\t\"Regex contains '.' which matches any character (except newline)\",\n888\t\t\t\t\t\"If you meant literal dot, escape it: '\\\\.'\",\n889\t\t\t\t\t\"If substring search is intended, consider using plain text without metacharacters\",\n890\t\t\t\t)\n891\t\t\t}\n892\t\n893\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"*\") && !strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \".*\") {\n894\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n895\t\t\t\t\t\"Regex contains bare '*' which is a quantifier, not a wildcard\",\n896\t\t\t\t\t\"For 'any characters' pattern, use: '.*' instead of '*'\",\n897\t\t\t\t\t\"For 'zero or more' of previous character, ensure '*' is preceded by pattern to repeat\",\n898\t\t\t\t)\n899\t\t\t}\n900\t\n901\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"^\") || strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"$\") {\n902\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n903\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern uses anchors (^ or $) which match start/end of string\",\n904\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure actual message doesn't have extra whitespace or newlines\",\n905\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider removing anchors if message may contain additional text\",\n906\t\t\t\t)\n907\t\t\t}\n908\t\n909\t\t\t// Case sensitivity check - detect if pattern and message have different casing\n910\t\t\texpectedHasMixedCase := expectedPattern != expectedLower\n911\t\t\tactualHasMixedCase := actualMessage != actualLower\n912\t\n913\t\t\t// Check if there's a case mismatch between pattern and message\n914\t\t\tif expectedHasMixedCase && actualHasMixedCase && expectedLower != actualLower {\n915\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n916\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern is case-sensitive but may need case-insensitive matching\",\n917\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using '(?i)' prefix for case-insensitive regex\",\n918\t\t\t\t\t\"Or convert pattern to handle different casing patterns\",\n919\t\t\t\t)\n920\t\t\t}\n921\t\t} else {\n922\t\t\t// Substring-specific suggestions\n923\t\t\tif expectedLower != actualLower && strings.Contains(actualLower, expectedLower) {\n924\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n925\t\t\t\t\t\"Substring match is case-sensitive\",\n926\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern was found but with different casing\",\n927\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using regex with '(?i)' prefix for case-insensitive matching\",\n928\t\t\t\t)\n929\t\t\t}\n930\t\n931\t\t\t// Check for word boundary issues\n932\t\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \" \") && strings.Contains(actualMessage, strings.ReplaceAll(expectedPattern, \" \", \"\")) {\n933\t\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n934\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern contains spaces but actual message may not\",\n935\t\t\t\t\t\"Consider using regex with '\\\\s*' to match optional whitespace\",\n936\t\t\t\t\t\"Or remove spaces from pattern for flexible matching\",\n937\t\t\t\t)\n938\t\t\t}\n939\t\t}\n940\t\n941\t\t// Pattern complexity suggestions\n942\t\tif len(expectedPattern) > 50 {\n943\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n944\t\t\t\t\"Pattern is quite long and complex\",\n945\t\t\t\t\"Consider breaking it into simpler patterns\",\n946\t\t\t\t\"Test pattern incrementally to identify which part fails\",\n947\t\t\t)\n948\t\t}\n949\t\n950\t\t// If no specific suggestions, provide generic ones\n951\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n952\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n953\t\t\t\t\"Review the expected pattern and actual message content\",\n954\t\t\t\t\"Check if pattern should be regex or substring matching\",\n955\t\t\t\t\"Verify the actual message format matches expectations\",\n956\t\t\t\t\"Consider using a more flexible pattern if multiple message formats are possible\",\n957\t\t\t)\n958\t\t}\n959\t\n960\t\treturn suggestions\n961\t}\n962\t\n963\t// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\n964\t// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\n965\tfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\n966\t\t// Convert to string and truncate\n967\t\tresponseStr := string(response)\n968\t\tmaxLen := 200\n969\t\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\n970\t\t\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \"...\"\n971\t\t}\n972\t\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\n973\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\n\", \" \")\n974\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\r\", \" \")\n975\t\treturn responseStr\n976\t}\n977\t\n978\t// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\n979\t//\n980\t// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\n981\t// - Custom field selection\n982\t// - Case-insensitive matching\n983\t// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\n984\t// - Multiple pattern checking\n985\t//\n986\t// Parameters:\n987\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n988\t//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\n989\t//\n990\t// Returns:\n991\t//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\n992\t//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\n993\t//\n994\t// Example usage:\n995\t//\n996\t//\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\n997\t//\t    Pattern:         \"unauthorized\",\n998\t//\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\n999\t//\t    FieldNames:      []string{\"error\", \"detail\"},\n1000\t//\t}\n1001\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\n1002\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\n1003\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1004\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1005\t\t}\n1006\t\n1007\t\tif config == nil {\n1008\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"config cannot be nil\")\n1009\t\t}\n1010\t\n1011\t\tif config.Pattern == \"\" {\n1012\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n1013\t\t}\n1014\t\n1015\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1016\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1017\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1018\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1019\t\t}\n1020\t\n1021\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n1022\t\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\n1023\t\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\n1024\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + config.Pattern\n1025\t\t}\n1026\t\n1027\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n1028\t\tif err != nil {\n1029\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n1030\t\t}\n1031\t\n1032\t\t// Determine which fields to check\n1033\t\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\n1034\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n1035\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n1036\t\t}\n1037\t\n1038\t\t// Check each specified field\n1039\t\tmatchedFields := []string{}\n1040\t\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\n1041\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1042\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1043\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n1044\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n1045\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n1046\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1047\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1048\t\t\t\t\t}\n1049\t\t\t\t}\n1050\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n1051\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1052\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n1053\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1054\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n1055\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n1056\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n1057\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1058\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1059\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1060\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1061\t\t\t\t\t}\n1062\t\t\t\t}\n1063\t\t\t}\n1064\t\t}\n1065\t\n1066\t\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\n1067\t\tif !config.MatchAny {\n1068\t\t\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\n1069\t\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t\treturn false, nil\n1072\t}\n1073\t\n1074\t// =============================================================================\n1075\t// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\n1076\t// =============================================================================\n1077\t\n1078\t// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\n1079\t//\n1080\t// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\n1081\t// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\n1082\t//\n1083\t// Parameters:\n1084\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1085\t//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"INVALID_TOKEN\", \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n1086\t//\n1087\t// Returns:\n1088\t//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\n1089\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1090\t//\n1091\t// Example usage:\n1092\t//\n1093\t//\t// Check for \"AUTH_FAILED\" error code\n1094\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n1095\t//\n1096\t//\t// Check for OAuth \"invalid_token\" error\n1097\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\")\n1098\tfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n1099\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1100\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1101\t\t}\n1102\t\n1103\t\tif expectedCode == \"\" {\n1104\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"expected code cannot be empty\")\n1105\t\t}\n1106\t\n1107\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1108\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1109\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1110\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1111\t\t}\n1112\t\n1113\t\t// Common error code field names\n1114\t\terrorCodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n1115\t\n1116\t\t// Check for error code in standard fields\n1117\t\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\n1118\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1119\t\t\t\t// Check for string match\n1120\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n1121\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n1122\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1123\t\t\t\t\t}\n1124\t\t\t\t}\n1125\t\t\t\t// Check for nested error code in error object\n1126\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1127\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n1128\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok {\n1129\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n1130\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1131\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1132\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1133\t\t\t\t\t}\n1134\t\t\t\t}\n1135\t\t\t}\n1136\t\t}\n1137\t\n1138\t\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\n1139\t\tif errorCode, exists := body[\"error\"]; exists {\n1140\t\t\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\n1141\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n1142\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n1143\t\t\t\t}\n1144\t\t\t}\n1145\t\t}\n1146\t\n1147\t\treturn false, nil\n1148\t}\n1149\t\n1150\t// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\n1151\t//\n1152\t// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\n1153\t// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\n1154\t// information are consistent.\n1155\t//\n1156\t// Parameters:\n1157\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1158\t//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP status code\n1159\t//   - expectedCode: Expected error code in response body\n1160\t//\n1161\t// Returns:\n1162\t//   - true if both status code and error code match, false otherwise\n1163\t//   - error if body parsing fails\n1164\t//\n1165\t// Example usage:\n1166\t//\n1167\t//\t// Validate 401 response with \"UNAUTHORIZED\" error code\n1168\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 401, \"UNAUTHORIZED\")\n1169\t//\n1170\t//\t// Validate 403 response with \"FORBIDDEN\" error code\n1171\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 403, \"FORBIDDEN\")\n1172\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n1173\t\tif resp == nil {\n1174\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response is nil\")\n1175\t\t}\n1176\t\n1177\t\t// Check status code\n1178\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n1179\t\t\treturn false, nil\n1180\t\t}\n1181\t\n1182\t\t// Read response body\n1183\t\tvar bodyBytes []byte\n1184\t\tif resp.Body != nil {\n1185\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n1186\t\t\tvar err error\n1187\t\t\tbodyBytes, err = io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n1188\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1189\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n1190\t\t\t}\n1191\t\t}\n1192\t\n1193\t\t// Check error code\n1194\t\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\n1195\t\tif err != nil {\n1196\t\t\treturn false, err\n1197\t\t}\n1198\t\n1199\t\treturn found, nil\n1200\t}\n1201\t\n1202\t// GetErrorMessage extracts the error message from a response body.\n1203\t//\n1204\t// This helper function searches for error messages in common fields and returns\n1205\t// the first non-empty message found.\n1206\t//\n1207\t// Parameters:\n1208\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1209\t//\n1210\t// Returns:\n1211\t//   - The extracted error message, or empty string if not found\n1212\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1213\t//\n1214\t// Example usage:\n1215\t//\n1216\t//\tmessage, err := GetErrorMessage(body)\n1217\t//\tif message != \"\" {\n1218\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error: %s\\n\", message)\n1219\t//\t}\n1220\tfunc GetErrorMessage(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n1221\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1222\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1223\t\t}\n1224\t\n1225\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1226\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1227\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1228\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1229\t\t}\n1230\t\n1231\t\t// Check common message fields\n1232\t\tmessageFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n1233\t\tfor _, field := nil\n1237\t\t\t\t}\n1238\t\t\t\t// Check nested message in error object\n1239\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1240\t\t\t\t\tif messageValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n1241\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := messageValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1242\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1243\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1244\t\t\t\t\t}\n1245\t\t\t\t}\n1246\t\t\t}\n1247\t\t}\n1248\t\n1249\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1250\t}\n1251\t\n1252\t// GetErrorCode extracts the error code from a response body.\n1253\t//\n1254\t// This helper function searches for error codes in common fields and returns\n1255\t// the first non-empty code found.\n1256\t//\n1257\t// Parameters:\n1258\t//   - bodyBytes: (string, error) {\n1271\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1272\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1273\t\t}\n1274\t\n1275\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1276\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1277\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1278\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1279\t\t}\n1280\t\n1281\t\t// Check common code fields\n1282\t\tcodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\"}\n1283\t\tfor _, field := range codeFields {\n1284\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1285\t\t\t\t// Direct string code\n1286\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1287\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1288\t\t\t\t}\n1289\t\t\t\t// Check nested
