codeValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1293\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1294\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1295\t\t\t\t\t}\n1296\t\t\t\t}\n1297\t\t\t}\n1298\t\t}\n1299\t\n1300\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1301\t}\n1302\t\n1303\t// =============================================================================\n1304\t// CONVENIENCE VALIDATION FUNCTIONS FOR EXAMPLES\n1305\t// =============================================================================\n1306\t\n1307\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\n1308\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\n1309\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\n1310\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n1311\t\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n1312\t\tValid bool\n1313\t\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n1314\t\tActualCode int\n1315\t\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\n1316\t\tExpectedCodes []int\n1317\t\t// MatchedCode is the specific expected code that matched (if any)\n1318\t\tMatchedCode *int\n1319\t\t// MismatchDetails contains human-readable information about why validation failed\n1320\t\tMismatchDetails string\n1321\t\t// IsClientError indicates whether the actual code is a client error (4xx)\n1322\t\tIsClientError bool\n1323\t\t// IsServerError indicates whether the actual code is a server error (5xx)\n1324\t\tIsServerError bool\n1325\t\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\n1326\t\tCategory string\n1327\t}\n1328\t\n1329\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\n1330\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\n1331\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\n1332\t//\n1333\t// Parameters:\n1334\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1335\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\n1336\t//   - requestContext: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"POST /api/users\", \"GET /api/resource/123\")\n1337\t//\n1338\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1339\t//\n1340\t// Example usage:\n1341\t//\n1342\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204}, \"POST /api/users\")\n1343\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1344\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %s\", result.MismatchDetails)\n1345\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Got: %d, expected one of: %v\", result.ActualCode, result.ExpectedCodes)\n1346\t//\t}\n1347\t//\n1348\t//\t// Single code validation without context\n1349\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, 200, \"\")\n1350\t//\tif result.Valid {\n1351\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code is valid: %d\", result.ActualCode)\n1352\t//\t}\n1353\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}, requestContext ...string) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1354\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1355\t\t\tValid:             false,\n1356\t\t\tActualCode:        0,\n1357\t\t\tExpectedCodes:     []int{},\n1358\t\t\tMatchedCode:       nil,\n1359\t\t\tMismatchDetails:   \"\",\n1360\t\t\tIsClientError:     false,\n1361\t\t\tIsServerError:     false,\n1362\t\t\tCategory:          \"unknown\",\n1363\t\t}\n1364\t\n1365\t\tif resp == nil {\n1366\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1367\t\t\treturn result\n1368\t\t}\n1369\t\n1370\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1371\t\n1372\t\t// Set error flags and category\n1373\t\tswitch {\n1374\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500:\n1375\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1376\t\t\tresult.Category = \"client_error\"\n1377\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600:\n1378\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1379\t\t\tresult.Category = \"server_error\"\n1380\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300:\n1381\t\t\tresult.Category = \"success\"\n1382\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 300 && resp.StatusCode < 400:\n1383\t\t\tresult.Category = \"redirection\"\n1384\t\tdefault:\n1385\t\t\tresult.Category = \"other\"\n1386\t\t}\n1387\t\n1388\t\t// Parse expected codes\n1389\t\tvar expectedCodes []int\n1390\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n1391\t\tcase int:\n1392\t\t\texpectedCodes = []int{v}\n1393\t\tcase []int:\n1394\t\t\texpectedCodes = v\n1395\t\tdefault:\n1396\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid expected type: %T\", expected)\n1397\t\t\treturn result\n1398\t\t}\n1399\t\n1400\t\tresult.ExpectedCodes = expectedCodes\n1401\t\n1402\t\t// Check for match\n1403\t\tfor _, code := range expectedCodes {\n1404\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == code {\n1405\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = true\n1406\t\t\t\tresult.MatchedCode = &code\n1407\t\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"\"\n1408\t\t\t\treturn result\n1409\t\t\t}\n1410\t\t}\n1411\t\n1412\t\t// Extract optional context\n1413\t\tcontext := \"\"\n1414\t\tif len(requestContext) > 0 && requestContext[0] != \"\" {\n1415\t\t\tcontext = requestContext[0]\n1416\t\t}\n1417\t\n1418\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch information with suggestions\n1419\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(\n1420\t\t\texpectedCodes, resp.StatusCode, result.Category, result.IsClientError, result.IsServerError, context)\n1421\t\n1422\t\treturn result\n1423\t}\n1424\t\n1425\t// buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails creates detailed mismatch information including\n1426\t// status code category, context, and specific suggestions based on the actual code.\n1427\t// It uses FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting across the codebase.\n1428\tfunc buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(expectedCodes []int, actualCode int, category string, isClientError, isServerError bool, context string) string {\n1429\t\t// Build validation details that include status code category information\n1430\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n1431\t\n1432\t\t// Add status code category information to validation details\n1433\t\tcategoryDesc := getCategoryDescription(category)\n1434\t\tif categoryDesc != \"\" {\n1435\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code category: %s\", categoryDesc))\n1436\t\t}\n1437\t\n1438\t\t// Prepare range info with category details\n1439\t\tvar rangeInfo string\n1440\t\tif isClientError {\n1441\t\t\trangeInfo = \"400-499 (Client Error)\"\n1442\t\t} else if isServerError {\n1443\t\t\trangeInfo = \"500-599 (Server Error)\"\n1444\t\t} else if category == \"success\" {\n1445\t\t\trangeInfo = \"200-299 (Success)\"\n1446\t\t} else if category == \"redirection\" {\n1447\t\t\trangeInfo = \"300-399 (Redirection)\"\n1448\t\t}\n1449\t\n1450\t\t// Determine expected value for FormatValidationErrorWithDetails\n1451\t\tvar expected interface{}\n1452\t\tif len(expectedCodes) == 1 {\n1453\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes[0]\n1454\t\t} else {\n1455\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes\n1456\t\t}\n1457\t\n1458\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent error formatting\n1459\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1460\t\t\t\"status_code\",\n1461\t\t\texpected,\n1462\t\t\tactualCode,\n1463\t\t\tcontext,\n1464\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1465\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1466\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1467\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1468\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails - not applicable for status code validation\n1469\t\t\trangeInfo,\n1470\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1471\t\t)\n1472\t\n1473\t\t// Return the formatted error string\n1474\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1475\t}\n1476\t\n1477\t// getCategoryDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code category.\n1478\tfunc getCategoryDescription(category string) string {\n1479\t\tdescriptions := map[string]string{\n1480\t\t\t\"success\":       \"2xx - Successful responses\",\n1481\t\t\t\"redirection\":   \"3xx - Redirection messages\",\n1482\t\t\t\"client_error\":  \"4xx - Client error responses\",\n1483\t\t\t\"server_error\":  \"5xx - Server error responses\",\n1484\t\t\t\"other\":         \"Non-standard status codes\",\n1485\t\t}\n1486\t\treturn descriptions[category]\n1487\t}\n1488\t\n1489\t// getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions returns relevant suggestions based on the specific status code received.\n1490\tfunc getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions(code int) []string {\n1491\t\tswitch code {\n1492\t\tcase 400:\n1493\t\t\treturn []string{\n1494\t\t\t\t\"Check request body syntax and formatting\",\n1495\t\t\t\t\"Verify all required fields are present\",\n1496\t\t\t\t\"Ensure Content-Type header matches request body format\",\n1497\t\t\t\t\"Validate JSON schema if applicable\",\n1498\t\t\t}\n1499\t\tcase 401:\n1500\t\t\treturn []string{\n1501\t\t\t\t\"Verify authentication credentials are correct\",\n1502\t\t\t\t\"Check if API token or session has expired\",\n1503\t\t\t\t\"Ensure Authorization header is properly formatted (e.g., 'Bearer <token>')\",\n1504\t\t\t\t\"Confirm authentication method is supported\",\n1505\t\t\t}\n1506\t\tcase 403:\n1507\t\t\treturn []string{\n1508\t\t\t\t\"Verify your account has permission to access this resource\",\n1509\t\t\t\t\"Check if additional scopes or roles are required\",\n1510\t\t\t\t\"Review API documentation for required permissions\",\n1511\t\t\t\t\"Ensure proper OAuth scopes are granted\",\n1512\t\t\t}\n1513\t\tcase 404:\n1514\t\t\treturn []string{\n1515\t\t\t\t\"Verify the endpoint URL is correct\",\n1516\t\t\t\t\"Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\",\n1517\t\t\t\t\"Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\",\n1518\t\t\t\t\"Review API versioning (URL path may have changed)\",\n1519\t\t\t}\n1520\t\tcase 409:\n1521\t\t\treturn []string{\n1522\t\t\t\t\"Check if a resource with this identifier already exists\",\n1523\t\t\t\t\"Verify unique constraints on the resource\",\n1524\t\t\t\t\"Consider using PUT instead of POST for updates\",\n1525\t\t\t\t\"Review state transition rules for the resource\",\n1526\t\t\t}\n1527\t\tcase 429:\n1528\t\t\treturn []string{\n1529\t\t\t\t\"Implement rate limiting and exponential backoff\",\n1530\t\t\t\t\"Check API quota limits and current usage\",\n1531\t\t\t\t\"Consider caching responses to reduce request frequency\",\n1532\t\t\t\t\"Review rate limit headers (Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Remaining)\",\n1533\t\t\t}\n1534\t\tcase 500:\n1535\t\t\treturn []string{\n1536\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\",\n1537\t\t\t\t\"Check service status page for ongoing issues\",\n1538\t\t\t\t\"Contact support if the issue persists\",\n1539\t\t\t\t\"Verify request doesn't trigger server-side bugs\",\n1540\t\t\t}\n1541\t\tcase 502:\n1542\t\t\treturn []string{\n1543\t\t\t\t\"Check if upstream services are operational\",\n1544\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry logic for transient failures\",\n1545\t\t\t\t\"Verify network connectivity to upstream services\",\n1546\t\t\t\t\"Review load balancer configuration\",\n1547\t\t\t}\n1548\t\tcase 503:\n1549\t\t\treturn []string{\n1550\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry with exponential backoff\",\n1551\t\t\t\t\"Check service maintenance schedules\",\n1552\t\t\t\t\"Verify service capacity and scaling\",\n1553\t\t\t\t\"Review circuit breaker patterns\",\n1554\t\t\t}\n1555\t\tcase 504:\n1556\t\t\treturn []string{\n1557\t\t\t\t\"Implement timeout and retry logic\",\n1558\t\t\t\t\"Check if upstream services are responding slowly\",\n1559\t\t\t\t\"Review request complexity and data size\",\n1560\t\t\t\t\"Verify network latency and connectivity\",\n1561\t\t\t}\n1562\t\tdefault:\n1563\t\t\t// Generic suggestions for other codes\n1564\t\t\tif code >= 400 && code < 500 {\n1565\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n1566\t\t\t\t\t\"Review request parameters and body\",\n1567\t\t\t\t\t\"Check API documentation for requirements\",\n1568\t\t\t\t\t\"Verify all headers are correctly set\",\n1569\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure request complies with API contracts\",\n1570\t\t\t\t}\n1571\t\t\t}\n1572\t\t\tif code >= 500 && code < 600 {\n1573\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n1574\t\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\",\n1575\t\t\t\t\t\"Check service status page for ongoing issues\",\n1576\t\t\t\t\t\"Contact support if the issue persists\",\n1577\t\t\t\t\t\"Review server logs if accessible\",\n1578\t\t\t\t}\n1579\t\t\t}\n1580\t\t}\n1581\t\treturn []string{}\n1582\t}\n1583\t\n1584\t// StatusCodeRange defines a range of status codes for flexible validation.\n1585\t// This is useful when you want to accept any status code within a certain range.\n1586\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n1587\t\t// Min is the minimum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1588\t\tMin int\n1589\t\t// Max is the maximum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1590\t\tMax int\n1591\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of what this range represents\n1592\t\tDescription string\n1593\t}\n1594\t\n1595\t// Common status code ranges\n1596\tvar (\n1597\t\t// Range1xx covers informational responses (100-199)\n1598\t\tRange1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n1599\t\t// Range2xx covers successful responses (200-299)\n1600\t\tRange2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n1601\t\t// Range3xx covers redirection responses (300-399)\n1602\t\tRange3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n1603\t\t// Range4xx covers client error responses (400-499)\n1604\t\tRange4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n1605\t\t// Range5xx covers server error responses (500-599)\n1606\t\tRange5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n1607\t)\n1608\t\n1609\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange checks if a response's status code falls within a specified range.\n1610\t// This is useful for flexible validation where you want to accept any status code within a category.\n1611\t//\n1612\t// Parameters:\n1613\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1614\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1615\t//\n1616\t// Returns true if the response status code falls within the range, false otherwise.\n1617\t// Returns false if the response is nil or the range is invalid (Min > Max).\n1618\t//\n1619\t// Example usage:\n1620\t//\n1621\t//\t// Accept any success code (2xx)\n1622\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range2xx) {\n1623\t//\t    // Response is successful (200-299)\n1624\t//\t}\n1625\t//\n1626\t//\t// Accept any client error (4xx)\n1627\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range4xx) {\n1628\t//\t    // Response is a client error (400-499)\n1629\t//\t}\n1630\t//\n1631\t//\t// Custom range for specific validation\n1632\t//\tcustomRange := StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 204, Description: \"Standard success\"}\n1633\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, customRange) {\n1634\t//\t    // Response is in custom range\n1635\t//\t}\n1636\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) bool {\n1637\t\tif resp == nil {\n1638\t\t\treturn false\n1639\t\t}\n1640\t\n1641\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1642\t\t\treturn false\n1643\t\t}\n1644\t\n1645\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1646\t}\n1647\t\n1648\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails provides detailed status code range validation.\n1649\t// Like ValidateStatusCodeRange but with comprehensive result information including\n1650\t// range boundaries, distance from valid range, and contextual suggestions.\n1651\t//\n1652\t// Parameters:\n1653\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1654\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1655\t//\n1656\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed information about the range validation,\n1657\t// including expected range boundaries, actual status code, distance from valid range,\n1658\t// range pattern context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx'), and suggestions for fixing the issue.\n1659\t//\n1660\t// Example usage:\n1661\t//\n1662\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(response, Range4xx)\n1663\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1664\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code %d is not in range %s (%d-%d)\",\n1665\t//\t        result.ActualCode, result.Category, range.Min, range.Max)\n1666\t//\t    // MismatchDetails now includes:\n1667\t//\t    // - Expected range with description (e.g., \"400-499 Client Error\")\n1668\t//\t    // - Actual received status code\n1669\t//\t    // - Distance from valid range (e.g., \"99 codes below range\")\n1670\t//\t    // - Range pattern context (e.g., \"Validating 4xx pattern\")\n1671\t//\t    // - Specific suggestions based on the actual code\n1672\t//\t}\n1673\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1674\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1675\t\t\tValid:           false,\n1676\t\t\tActualCode:      0,\n1677\t\t\tExpectedCodes:   []int{statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max},\n1678\t\t\tMatchedCode:     nil,\n1679\t\t\tMismatchDetails: \"\",\n1680\t\t\tIsClientError:   false,\n1681\t\t\tIsServerError:   false,\n1682\t\t\tCategory:        statusRange.Description,\n1683\t\t}\n1684\t\n1685\t\tif resp == nil {\n1686\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1687\t\t\treturn result\n1688\t\t}\n1689\t\n1690\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1691\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid range: Min (%d) > Max (%d)\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1692\t\t\treturn result\n1693\t\t}\n1694\t\n1695\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1696\t\n1697\t\t// Check range\n1698\t\tinRange := resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1699\t\tresult.Valid = inRange\n1700\t\n1701\t\t// Set error flags\n1702\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500 {\n1703\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1704\t\t}\n1705\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600 {\n1706\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1707\t\t}\n1708\t\n1709\t\tif !inRange {\n1710\t\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch details with range boundaries, distance, and suggestions\n1711\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildRangeMismatchDetails(resp.StatusCode, statusRange)\n1712\t\t}\n1713\t\n1714\t\treturn result\n1715\t}\n1716\t\n1717\t// buildRangeMismatchDetails creates comprehensive mismatch information for range validation failures.\n1718\t// This function calculates the distance from the valid range, identifies the range pattern context,\n1719\t// and generates detailed error messages with suggestions using FormatValidationErrorWithDetails.\n1720\tfunc buildRangeMismatchDetails(actualCode int, statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1721\t\t// Calculate distance from valid range\n1722\t\tvar distanceInfo string\n1723\t\tvar distanceDetails []string\n1724\t\n1725\t\tif actualCode < statusRange.Min {\n1726\t\t\tdistance := statusRange.Min - actualCode\n1727\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1728\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code below range\", distance)\n1729\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1730\t\t\t} else {\n1731\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes below range\", distance)\n1732\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1733\t\t\t}\n1734\t\t} else {\n1735\t\t\tdistance := actualCode - statusRange.Max\n1736\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1737\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code above range\", distance)\n1738\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1739\t\t\t} else {\n1740\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes above range\", distance)\n1741\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1742\t\t\t}\n1743\t\t}\n1744\t\n1745\t\t// Determine range pattern context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n1746\t\trangePattern := getRangePattern(statusRange)\n1747\t\n1748\t\t// Build expected range with description\n1749\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1750\t\tif statusRange.Description != \"\" {\n1751\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", expectedRange, statusRange.Description)\n1752\t\t}\n1753\t\n1754\t\t// Add pattern context to expected range\n1755\t\tif rangePattern != \"\" {\n1756\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s - %s pattern\", expectedRange, rangePattern)\n1757\t\t}\n1758\t\n1759\t\t// Build validation details\n1760\t\tvalidationDetails := []string{\n1761\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d %s\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max, statusRange.Description),\n1762\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actualCode),\n1763\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n1764\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", rangePattern),\n1765\t\t}\n1766\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, distanceDetails...)\n1767\t\n1768\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting\n1769\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1770\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1771\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1772\t\t\tactualCode,\n1773\t\t\t\"\", // context - not applicable for range validation\n1774\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1775\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1776\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1777\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1778\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern: %s, Distance: %s\", rangePattern, distanceInfo),\n1779\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1780\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1781\t\t)\n1782\t\n1783\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1784\t}\n1785\t\n1786\t// getRangePattern identifies the range pattern from a StatusCodeRange.\n1787\t// Returns patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '2xx', etc. based on the range boundaries.\n1788\tfunc getRangePattern(statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1789\t\tmin := statusRange.Min\n1790\t\tmax := statusRange.Max\n1791\t\n1792\t\t// Check for standard century ranges\n1793\t\tif min/100 == max/100 && min%100 == 0 && max%100 == 99 {\n1794\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1795\t\t\tswitch century {\n1796\t\t\tcase 1:\n1797\t\t\t\treturn \"1xx (Informational)\"\n1798\t\t\tcase 2:\n1799\t\t\t\treturn \"2xx (Success)\"\n1800\t\t\tcase 3:\n1801\t\t\t\treturn \"3xx (Redirection)\"\n1802\t\t\tcase 4:\n1803\t\t\t\treturn \"4xx (Client Error)\"\n1804\t\t\tcase 5:\n1805\t\t\t\treturn \"5xx (Server Error)\"\n1806\t\t\t}\n1807\t\t}\n1808\t\n1809\t\t// For custom ranges, provide the pattern context\n1810\t\tif min/100 == max/100 {\n1811\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1812\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%dxx range (custom)\", century)\n1813\t\t}\n1814\t\n1815\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d range (custom)\", min/100, max/100)\n1816\t}\n1817\t\n1818\t// =============================================================================\n1819\t// ENHANCED ERROR MESSAGE VALIDATION\n1820\t// =============================================================================\n1821\t\n1822\t// EnhancedErrorMessagePattern defines a pattern for matching error messages in response bodies.\n1823\ttype EnhancedErrorMessagePattern struct {\n1824\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check in the response body\n1825\t\tFieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames\n1826\t\t// Pattern is a regular expression pattern that the error message must match\n1827\t\tPattern string\n1828\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether pattern matching should be case-insensitive\n1829\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n1830\t\t// MustContain specifies strings that must be present in the error message\n1831\t\tMustContain []string\n1832\t\t// MustNotContain specifies strings that must NOT be present in the error message\n1833\t\tMustNotContain []string\n1834\t\t// MinLength is the minimum length of the error message (0 for no minimum)\n1835\t\tMinLength int\n1836\t\t// MaxLength is the maximum length of the error message (0 for no maximum)\n1837\t\tMaxLength int\n1838\t}\n1839\t\n1840\t// ErrorMessageValidationResult provides detailed information about error message validation.\n1841\ttype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n1842\t\t// Valid indicates whether the error message validation passed\n1843\t\tValid bool\n1844\t\t// Found indicates whether an error message field was found in the response\n1845\t\tFound bool\n1846\t\t// Message is the actual error message content that was found\n1847\t\tMessage string\n1848\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error message was found\n1849\t\tFieldName string\n1850\t\t// PatternMatched indicates whether the regex pattern matched\n1851\t\tPatternMatched bool\n1852\t\t// MustContainResults shows which required strings were found\n1853\t\tMustContainResults map[string]bool\n1854\t\t// MustNotContainResults shows which forbidden strings were found (should all be false)\n1855\t\tMustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n1856\t\t// LengthValidation indicates whether the message length was valid\n1857\t\tLengthValidation bool\n1858\t\t// Issues contains a list of validation issues found\n1859\t\tIssues []string\n1860\t}\n1861\t\n1862\t// ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails performs comprehensive error message content validation.\n1863\t// It checks for the presence of error messages and validates their content against various criteria.\n1864\t//\n1865\t// Parameters:\n1866\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n1867\t//   - pattern: The validation pattern to apply\n1868\t//\n1869\t// Returns an ErrorMessageValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1870\t//\n1871\t// Example usage:\n1872\t//\n1873\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1874\t//\tpattern := EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n1875\t//\t    FieldNames: DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames(),\n1876\t//\t    MustContain: []string{\"invalid\", \"token\"},\n1877\t//\t    MustNotContain: []string{\"password\", \"secret\"},\n1878\t//\t    MinLength: 10,\n1879\t//\t}\n1880\t//\tresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(body, pattern)\n1881\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1882\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Error message validation failed: %v\", result.Issues)\n1883\t//\t}\n1884\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody interface{}, pattern EnhancedErrorMessagePattern) ErrorMessageValidationResult {\n1885\t\tresult := ErrorMessageValidationResult{\n1886\t\t\tValid:                 false,\n1887\t\t\tFound:                 false,\n1888\t\t\tMessage:               \"\",\n1889\t\t\tFieldName:             \"\",\n1890\t\t\tPatternMatched:        false,\n1891\t\t\tMustContainResults:    make(map[string]bool),\n1892\t\t\tMustNotContainResults: make(map[string]bool),\n1893\t\t\tLengthValidation:      true,\n1894\t\t\tIssues:                []string{},\n1895\t\t}\n1896\t\n1897\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1898\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is nil\")\n1899\t\t\treturn result\n1900\t\t}\n1901\t\n1902\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n1903\t\tfieldNames := pattern.FieldNames\n1904\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n1905\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n1906\t\t}\n1907\t\n1908\t\t// Try to convert to map\n1909\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n1910\t\tif !ok {\n1911\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is not a map\")\n1912\t\t\treturn result\n1913\t\t}\n1914\t\n1915\t\t// Search for error message in specified fields\n1916\t\tvar foundMessage string\n1917\t\tvar foundFieldName string\n1918\t\n1919\t\t// Check primary field\n1920\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1921\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n1922\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1923\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1924\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName\n1925\t\t\t\t}\n1926\t\t\t}\n1927\t\t}\n1928\t\n1929\t\t// Check secondary field if not found in primary\n1930\t\tif foundMessage == \"\" && fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1931\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n1932\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1933\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1934\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName\n1935\t\t\t\t}\n1936\t\t\t}\n1937\t\t}\n1938\t\n1939\t\tresult.Found = foundMessage != \"\"\n1940\t\tresult.Message = foundMessage\n1941\t\tresult.FieldName = foundFieldName\n1942\t\n1943\t\tif !result.Found {\n1944\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"no valid error message field found\")\n1945\t\t\treturn result\n1946\t\t}\n1947\t\n1948\t\t// Validate length\n1949\t\tif pattern.MinLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) < pattern.MinLength {\n1950\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1951\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d is less than minimum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MinLength))\n1952\t\t}\n1953\t\n1954\t\tif pattern.MaxLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) > pattern.MaxLength {\n1955\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1956\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d exceeds maximum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MaxLength))\n1957\t\t}\n1958\t\n1959\t\t// Validate regex pattern\n1960\t\tif pattern.Pattern != \"\" {\n1961\t\t\tregex := regexp.MustCompile(pattern.Pattern)\n1962\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1963\t\t\t\tregex = regexp.MustCompile(\"(?i)\" + pattern.Pattern)\n1964\t\t\t}\n1965\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = regex.MatchString(foundMessage)\n1966\t\t\tif !result.PatternMatched {\n1967\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not match required pattern: %s\", pattern.Pattern))\n1968\t\t\t}\n1969\t\t} else {\n1970\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\n1971\t\t}\n1972\t\n1973\t\t// Validate MustContain strings\n1974\t\tallMustContainFound := true\n1975\t\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\n1976\t\t\tvar found bool\n1977\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1978\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1979\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\n1980\t\t\t} else {\n1981\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\n1982\t\t\t}\n1983\t\t\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\n1984\t\t\tif !found {\n1985\t\t\t\tallMustContainFound = false\n1986\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not contain required string: %s\", mustContain))\n1987\t\t\t}\n1988\t\t}\n1989\t\n1990\t\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\n1991\t\thasForbiddenStrings := false\n1992\t\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\n1993\t\t\tvar found bool\n1994\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1995\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1996\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\n1997\t\t\t} else {\n1998\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\n1999\t\t\t}\n2000\t\t\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\n2001\t\t\tif found {\n2002\t\t\t\thasForbiddenStrings = true\n2003\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message contains forbidden string: %s\", mustNotContain))\n2004\t\t\t}\n2005\t\t}\n2006\t\n2007\t\t// Overall validity\n2008\t\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\n2009\t\t\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\n2010\t\n2011\t\treturn result\n2012\t}\n2013\t\n2014\t// =============================================================================\n2015\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\n2016\t// =============================================================================\n2017\t\n2018\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\n2019\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\n2020\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error code was found\n2021\t\tFieldName string\n2022\t\t// CodeValue is the actual error code value (as a string for flexibility)\n2023\t\tCodeValue string\n2024\t\t// NumericCode is the error code parsed as an integer (if applicable)\n2025\t\tNumericCode *int\n2026\t\t// MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched this code\n2027\t\tMatchedPattern string\n2028\t\t// Position describes where in the response the code was found\n2029\t\tPosition string\n2030\t}\n2031\t\n2032\t// EnhancedErrorCodePattern defines a pattern for finding error codes in responses.\n2033\ttype EnhancedErrorCodePattern struct {\n2034\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check for error codes\n2035\t\tFieldNames []string\n2036\t\t// NumericOnly indicates whether to only match numeric error codes\n2037\t\tNumericOnly bool\n2038\t\t// ValuePatterns contains regex patterns for valid error code values\n2039\t\tValuePatterns []string\n2040\t}\n2041\t\n2042\t// FindErrorCodesInResponse searches for error codes in an HTTP response body.\n2043\t// This is useful for validating that specific error codes are present in error responses.\n2044\t//\n2045\t// Parameters:\n2046\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n2047\t//   - patterns: The patterns to use for finding error codes\n2048\t//\n2049\t// Returns a slice of ErrorCodeMatch representing all found error codes.\n2050\t//\n2051\t// Example usage:\n2052\t//\n2053\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n2054\t//\tpatterns := []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2055\t//\t    {\n2056\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\"},\n2057\t//\t        NumericOnly: true,\n2058\t//\t    },\n2059\t//\t    {\n2060\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error\"},\n2061\t//\t        ValuePatterns: []string{\"invalid_token\", \"access_denied\"},\n2062\t//\t    },\n2063\t//\t}\n2064\t//\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(body, patterns)\n2065\t//\tfor _, match := range matches {\n2066\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Found error code: %s in field: %s\", match.CodeValue, match.FieldName)\n2067\t//\t}\n2068\tfunc FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody interface{}, patterns []EnhancedErrorCodePattern) []ErrorCodeMatch {\n2069\t\tvar matches []ErrorCodeMatch\n2070\t\n2071\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n2072\t\t\treturn matches\n2073\t\t}\n2074\t\n2075\t\t// Try to convert to map\n2076\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n2077\t\tif !ok {\n2078\t\t\treturn matches\n2079\t\t}\n2080\t\n2081\t\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\n2082\t\t\t// Default to common error code fields if none specified\n2083\t\t\tfieldsToCheck := pattern.FieldNames\n2084\t\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n2085\t\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n2086\t\t\t}\n2087\t\n2088\t\t\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\n2089\t\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\n2090\t\t\t\t\tvar codeValue string\n2091\t\t\t\t\tvar numericCode *int\n2092\t\t\t\t\tvar matchedPattern string\n2093\t\n2094\t\t\t\t\t// Handle different value types\n2095\t\t\t\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n2096\t\t\t\t\tcase more expected codes.\n// It supports both a single status code and a slice of valid status codes, making it useful for\n// validating API responses where multiple status codes might indicate success (e.g., 200 OK and 206 Partial Content).\n//\n// The expected parameter can be:\n//   - A single int (e.g., 200)\n//   - A slice of ints (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code matches any of the expected codes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tsingleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 200)\n//\tmultipleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\tnoContentCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tstatusCode := resp.StatusCode\n\n\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn statusCode == v\n\tcase []int:\n\t\tfor _, code := range v {\n\t\t\tif statusCode == code {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-599 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle error response\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a client error (4xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-499 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a server error (5xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 500-599 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle 5xx server error (internal server error, bad gateway, etc.)\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n}\n\n// ContentTypeIsValid checks if a response's Content-Type header matches the expected pattern.\n// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\n// For example, \"application/json\" will match \"application/json; charset=utf-8\".\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//\n// Returns true if the response Content-Type matches the expected pattern, false otherwise.\n// Returns false if the response is nil or has no Content-Type header.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif ContentTypeIsValid(response, \"application/json\") {\n//\t    // Handle JSON response\n//\t}\nfunc ContentTypeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected string) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tactual := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif actual == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse both content-type strings to extract base media types\n\tactualMediaType := parseContentType(actual)\n\texpectedMediaType := parseContentType(expected)\n\n\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\n}\n\n// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\n// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\n// For example, \"application/json; charset=utf-8\" becomes \"application/json\".\nfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\n\t// The first part before any semicolon is the base media type\n\tfor i, c := range contentType {\n\t\tif c == ';' {\n\t\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType[:i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No parameters found, return the whole string trimmed\n\treturn trimSpace(contentType)\n}\n\n// trimSpace removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.\nfunc trimSpace(s string) string {\n\tstart := 0\n\tend := len(s)\n\n\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\t' || s[start] == '\\n' || s[start] == '\\r') {\n\t\tstart++\n\t}\n\n\tfor end > start && (s[end-1] == ' ' || s[end-1] == '\\t' || s[end-1] == '\\n' || s[end-1] == '\\r') {\n\t\tend--\n\t}\n\n\treturn s[start:end]\n}\n\n// ErrorResponseFieldNames specifies custom field names to check when validating error response structures.\n// If nil, the default field names \"error\" and \"message\" are checked.\ntype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n\t// PrimaryFieldName is the main error field to check (e.g., \"error\", \"detail\", \"error_description\")\n\tPrimaryFieldName string\n\t// SecondaryFieldName is an optional secondary field to check (e.g., \"message\", \"description\")\n\tSecondaryFieldName string\n}\n\n// DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames returns the default field names used for error response validation.\n// This checks for both \"error\" and \"message\" fields.\nfunc DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames() *ErrorResponseFieldNames {\n\treturn &ErrorResponseFieldNames{\n\t\tPrimaryFieldName:   \"error\",\n\t\tSecondaryFieldName: \"message\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorResponseStructureIsValid validates whether a response object contains a valid error response structure.\n// It checks for the presence of common error fields and returns whether the structure is valid.\n//\n// The response parameter can be:\n//   - map[string]interface{}: A parsed JSON object representing the response body\n//   - Any type that can be inspected for error fields using reflection\n//\n// The fieldNames parameter allows customization of which fields to check:\n//   - If nil, checks for default fields \"error\" and \"message\"\n//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\n//\n// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\n// false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Using default field names (checks for \"error\" and \"message\")\n//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"Invalid input\"}\n//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\n//\n//\t// Using custom field names\n//\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \"detail\", SecondaryFieldName: \"description\"}\n//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"detail\": \"Resource not found\"}\n//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\nfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\n\tif response == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Use default field names if not provided\n\tif fieldNames == nil {\n\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\n\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check primary field\n\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check secondary field\n\tif fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\n// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\n// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\nfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\n\tif value == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase string:\n\t\treturn v != \"\"\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int8:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int16:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int32:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int64:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint8:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint16:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint32:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint64:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase float32:\n\t\treturn v != 0.0\n\tcase float64:\n\t\treturn v != 0.0\n\tcase bool:\n\t\treturn v\n\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n}\n\n// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\ntype CORSConfig struct {\n\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\n\t// Use \"*\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \"https://example.com\".\n\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\n\tAllowOrigin string\n\n\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\n\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\n\tAllowMethods string\n\n\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\n\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\n\tAllowHeaders string\n\n\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\n\t// If true, expects \"true\"; if false, expects \"true\" or header not present.\n\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\n\tAllowCredentials bool\n\n\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\n\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\n\tExposeHeaders string\n\n\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\n\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n\tMaxAge string\n}\n\n// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n//\n// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n//\n// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n// Returns false if the response is nil.\n//\n// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n//\t    // CORS headers are present\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate specific origin\n//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n//\t}\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n//\t}\nfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n\tif config == nil {\n\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\ntype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n\tPattern string\n\n\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n\tCaseInsensitive bool\n\n\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n\tMatchAny bool\n\n\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n\tFieldNames []string\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n//\n// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n//\n//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\tif pattern == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse JSON body\n\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compile regex pattern\n\tregexPattern := pattern\n\tif caseInsensitive {\n\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n\t}\n\n\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check default error fields\n\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n//\n// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n//\n// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Regex pattern matching\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    // Pattern not found\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Simple substring matching\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    // Substring not found\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Common error patterns\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n//\n// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n//\n// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n//\n// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Authentication Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Authorization Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Validation Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"validation.*failed\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*input\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Resource Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Resource not found\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not found\")  // substring match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"User does not exist\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"does not exist\")  // substring match\n//\n// # Rate Limiting Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"rate.*limit\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Too many requests, please try again later\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"too many requests\")  // substring match\n//\n// # Timeout Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Request timeout\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n//\n// # Server Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"internal.*error\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Service temporarily unavailable\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"unavailable\")  // substring match\nfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\n\t// Validate inputs\n\tif len(response) == 0 {\n\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\t\tWithExpected(\"non-empty response\").\n\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"cannot validate against empty response\").\n\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n\t\t\t\t\"Response body is empty\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Cannot validate error message pattern against empty response\",\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n\t\t\t\t\"Response body is empty\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Cannot validate error message pattern against empty response\",\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tFormat()\n\t}\n\n\tif expectedPattern == \"\" {\n\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\t\tWithExpected(\"non-empty pattern\").\n\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"pattern cannot be empty\").\n\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern cannot be empty\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Provide a pattern to match against error messages\",\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern cannot be empty\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Provide a pattern to match against error messages\",\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tFormat()\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse JSON body\n\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\n\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\t\tWithExpected(\"valid JSON\").\n\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"failed to parse response body\").\n\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err)).\n\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n\t\t\t\t\"Failed to parse response body\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err),\n\t\t\t\t\"Ensure response contains valid JSON\",\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tFormat()\n\t}\n\n\t// Search for error messages in common fields\n\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n\ttype foundMessage struct {\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t}\n\tvar foundMessages []foundMessage\n\n\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, foundMessage{\n\t\t\t\t\tmessage:   strValue,\n\t\t\t\t\tfieldName: field,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, foundMessage{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmessage:   strValue,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName: field + \".message\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If no error messages found, return detailed error with response snippet\n\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\n\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n\t\tresponseSize := len(response)\n\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\t\tWithExpected(\"error message field\").\n\t\t\tWithActual(\"\").\n\t\t\tWithPatternDetails(\"no error message found in response\").\n\t\t\tWithResponseSnippet(snippet).\n\t\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Response size: %d bytes\", responseSize),\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tWithSuggestions(\n\t\t\t\t\"No error message found in response body\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n\t\t\t\t\"Response may not contain standard error fields\",\n\t\t\t).\n\t\t\tFormat()\n\t}\n\n\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\n\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\n\n\t// Check each found message for pattern match\n\tfor _, foundMsg := range foundMessages {\n\t\tvar matched bool\n\n\t\tif isRegex {\n\t\t\t// Try regex matching\n\t\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"valid regex pattern '%s'\", expectedPattern),\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid regex '%s'\", expectedPattern),\n\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfoundMsg.fieldName,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n\t\t\t\t\tnil,\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern type: regex (contains metacharacters), Parse error: %v\", err),\n\t\t\t\t\t\"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t[]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid regex pattern: %s\", expectedPattern),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Regex compilation error: %v\", err),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Check regex syntax and escape special characters\",\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmatched = re.MatchString(foundMsg.message)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\n\t\t\tmatched = strings.Contains(foundMsg.message, expectedPattern)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif matched {\n\t\t\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error with full details\n\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\n\n\t// Build validation details including response size context\n\tresponseSize := len(response)\n\tvar validationDetails []string\n\tif isRegex {\n\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, \"Searched for regex pattern\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, \"Searched for substring\")\n\t}\n\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected pattern: %s\", expectedPattern))\n\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual message: \\\"%s\\\"\", firstMessage.message))\n\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Field name: %s\", firstMessage.fieldName))\n\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")))\n\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Response size: %d bytes\", responseSize))\n\n\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\n\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Found %d error message fields in response\", len(foundMessages)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate pattern-specific suggestions\n\tsuggestions := generatePatternMismatchSuggestions(expectedPattern, firstMessage.message, isRegex)\n\n\t// Build pattern details\n\tvar patternDetails string\n\tif isRegex {\n\t\tpatternDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"regex pattern '%s' did not match\", expectedPattern)\n\t} else {\n\t\tpatternDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"substring '%s' did not match\", expectedPattern)\n\t}\n\n\t// Use ValidationFormatter builder pattern for enhanced error formatting\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n\t\tWithActual(firstMessage.message).\n\t\tWithPatternDetails(patternDetails).\n\t\tWithResponseSnippet(snippet).\n\t\tWithFieldName(firstMessage.fieldName).\n\t\tWithContext(\"error message validation\").\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(validationDetails...).\n\t\tWithSuggestions(suggestions...).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\n// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\nfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\n\tregexChars := []string{\".\", \"*\", \"+\", \"?\", \"^\", \"$\", \"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \"(\", \")\", \"|\", \"\\\\\"}\n\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// generatePatternMismatchSuggestions provides relevant suggestions based on pattern and actual message.\n// This function analyzes the expected pattern and actual message to generate specific suggestions.\nfunc generatePatternMismatchSuggestions(expectedPattern, actualMessage string, isRegex bool) []string {\n\tvar suggestions []string\n\n\texpectedLower := strings.ToLower(expectedPattern)\n\tactualLower := strings.ToLower(actualMessage)\n\n\t// Common pattern mismatch checks\n\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"token\") && !strings.Contains(actualLower, \"token\") {\n\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\"Pattern looks for 'token' but actual message doesn't contain it\",\n\t\t\t\"Check if the error is about authentication/authorization rather than tokens\",\n\t\t\t\"Consider expanding pattern to include 'auth' or 'access' related terms\",\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"invalid\") && !strings.Contains(actualLower, \"invalid\") {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(actualLower, \"expired\") {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern expects 'invalid' but message contains 'expired'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Consider using pattern: 'invalid.*expired|expired.*invalid' to match both cases\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Or use separate patterns for different token states\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"not found\") && strings.Contains(actualLower, \"does not exist\") {\n\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\"Pattern uses 'not found' but message uses 'does not exist'\",\n\t\t\t\"Consider using pattern: 'not found|does not exist' to match both phrasings\",\n\t\t\t\"Or use simpler pattern: 'not.*found|does.*not.*exist'\",\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\tif isRegex {\n\t\t// Regex-specific suggestions\n\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \".\") && !strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"\\\\\") {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Regex contains '.' which matches any character (except newline)\",\n\t\t\t\t\"If you meant literal dot, escape it: '\\\\.'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"If substring search is intended, consider using plain text without metacharacters\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"*\") && !strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \".*\") {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Regex contains bare '*' which is a quantifier, not a wildcard\",\n\t\t\t\t\"For 'any characters' pattern, use: '.*' instead of '*'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"For 'zero or more' of previous character, ensure '*' is preceded by pattern to repeat\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"^\") || strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \"$\") {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern uses anchors (^ or $) which match start/end of string\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Ensure actual message doesn't have extra whitespace or newlines\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Consider removing anchors if message may contain additional text\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Case sensitivity check - detect if pattern and message have different casing\n\t\texpectedHasMixedCase := expectedPattern != expectedLower\n\t\tactualHasMixedCase := actualMessage != actualLower\n\n\t\t// Check if there's a case mismatch between pattern and message\n\t\tif expectedHasMixedCase && actualHasMixedCase && expectedLower != actualLower {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern is case-sensitive but may need case-insensitive matching\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Consider using '(?i)' prefix for case-insensitive regex\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Or convert pattern to handle different casing patterns\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Substring-specific suggestions\n\t\tif expectedLower != actualLower && strings.Contains(actualLower, expectedLower) {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Substring match is case-sensitive\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern was found but with different casing\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Consider using regex with '(?i)' prefix for case-insensitive matching\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for word boundary issues\n\t\tif strings.Contains(expectedPattern, \" \") && strings.Contains(actualMessage, strings.ReplaceAll(expectedPattern, \" \", \"\")) {\n\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern contains spaces but actual message may not\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Consider using regex with '\\\\s*' to match optional whitespace\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Or remove spaces from pattern for flexible matching\",\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern complexity suggestions\n\tif len(expectedPattern) > 50 {\n\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\"Pattern is quite long and complex\",\n\t\t\t\"Consider breaking it into simpler patterns\",\n\t\t\t\"Test pattern incrementally to identify which part fails\",\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\t// If no specific suggestions, provide generic ones\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n\t\t\t\"Review the expected pattern and actual message content\",\n\t\t\t\"Check if pattern should be regex or substring matching\",\n\t\t\t\"Verify the actual message format matches expectations\",\n\t\t\t\"Consider using a more flexible pattern if multiple message formats are possible\",\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\treturn suggestions\n}\n\n// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\n// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\nfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\n\t// Convert to string and truncate\n\tresponseStr := string(response)\n\tmaxLen := 200\n\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\n\t\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \"...\"\n\t}\n\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\n\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\n\", \" \")\n\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\r\", \" \")\n\treturn responseStr\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\n//\n// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\n// - Custom field selection\n// - Case-insensitive matching\n// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\n// - Multiple pattern checking\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\n//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\n//\t    Pattern:         \"unauthorized\",\n//\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\n//\t    FieldNames:      []string{\"error\", \"detail\"},\n//\t}\n//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\n\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\tif config == nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"config cannot be nil\")\n\t}\n\n\tif config.Pattern == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse JSON body\n\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compile regex pattern\n\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\n\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\n\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + config.Pattern\n\t}\n\n\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Determine which fields to check\n\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\n\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check each specified field\n\tmatchedFields := []string{}\n\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\n\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\n\tif !config.MatchAny {\n\t\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\n//\n// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\n// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"INVALID_TOKEN\", \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\n//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Check for \"AUTH_FAILED\" error code\n//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n//\n//\t// Check for OAuth \"invalid_token\" error\n//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\")\nfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\tif expectedCode == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"expected code cannot be empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse JSON body\n\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Common error code field names\n\terrorCodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n\n\t// Check for error code in standard fields\n\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\n\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n\t\t\t// Check for string match\n\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check for nested error code in error object\n\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\n\tif errorCode, exists := body[\"error\"]; exists {\n\t\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\n\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\n// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\n//\n// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\n// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\n// information are consistent.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP status code\n//   - expectedCode: Expected error code in response body\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if both status code and error code match, false otherwise\n//   - error if body parsing fails\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Validate 401 response with \"UNAUTHORIZED\" error code\n//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 401, \"UNAUTHORIZED\")\n//\n//\t// Validate 403 response with \"FORBIDDEN\" error code\n//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 403, \"FORBIDDEN\")\nfunc ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response is nil\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check status code\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Read response body\n\tvar bodyBytes []byte\n\tif resp.Body != nil {\n\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tbodyBytes, err = io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check error code\n\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn found, nil\n}\n\n// GetErrorMessage extracts the error message from a response body.\n//\n// This helper function searches for error messages in common fields and returns\n// the first non-empty message found.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - The extracted error message, or empty string if not found\n//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmessage, err := GetErrorMessage(body)\n//\tif message != \"\" {\n//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error: %s\\n\", message)\n//\t}\nfunc GetErrorMessage(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse JSON body\n\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check common message fields\n\tmessageFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n\tfor _, field := range messageFields {\n\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check nested message in error object\n\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif messageValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := messageValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\", nil\n}\n\n// GetErrorCode extracts the error code from a response body.\n//\n// This helper function searches for error codes in common fields and returns\n// the first non-empty code found.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - The extracted error code, or empty string if not
